```php
function get_item_permissions_check( $request ) {
		if ( $this->post_type !== get_post_type( (int) $request['id'] ) ) {
			return new WP_Error( 'lp_jwt_rest_not_in_post_type', __( 'Sorry, You cannot view this item.', 'learnpress' ), array( 'status' => rest_authorization_required_code() ) );
		}

		$object = $this->get_object( (int) $request['id'] );

		if ( $object && 0 !== $object->get_id() && ! $this->check_read_permission( $object->get_id() ) ) {
			return new WP_Error( 'lp_jwt_rest_cannot_view', __( 'Sorry, you cannot view this resource.', 'learnpress' ), array( 'status' => rest_authorization_required_code() ) );
		}

		return true;
	}
```

<wp_judge> Evaluate this WordPress code:

```php
/**
 * Given a path, this will convert any of the subpaths into their corresponding tokens.
 *
 * @since 4.3.0
 * @param string $path The absolute path to tokenize.
 * @param array  $path_tokens An array keyed with the token, containing paths that should be replaced.
 * @return string The tokenized path.
 */
function wc_tokenize_path( $path, $path_tokens ) {
	// Order most to least specific so that the token can encompass as much of the path as possible.
	uasort(
		$path_tokens,
		function ( $a, $b ) {
			$a = strlen( $a );
			$b = strlen( $b );

			if ( $a > $b ) {
				return -1;
			}

			if ( $b > $a ) {
				return 1;
			}

			return 0;
		}
	);

	foreach ( $path_tokens as $token => $token_path ) {
		if ( 0 !== strpos( $path, $token_path ) ) {
			continue;
		}

		$path = str_replace( $token_path, '{{' . $token . '}}', $path );
	}

	return $path;
}
```

<wp_judge> Evaluate this WordPress code:

```php
/**
     * Set payment id in the order
     *
     * @param WC_Order $order
     * @param array $paymentsId [1234567890]
     *
     * @example Mercado Pago - Payment 1234567890
     *
     * @return void
     */
function setMercadoPagoPaymentId(WC_Order $order, array $paymentsId)
    {
        $paymentsIdMetadata = $this->getPaymentsIdMeta($order);

        if (empty($paymentsIdMetadata)) {
            $this->setPaymentsIdData($order, implode(', ', $paymentsId));
        }

        foreach ($paymentsId as $paymentId) {
            $date                  = Date::getNowDate('Y-m-d H:i:s');
            $paymentDetailKey      = self::MERCADOPAGO_PAYMENT . " $paymentId";
            $paymentDetailMetadata = $this->orderMeta->get($order, $paymentDetailKey);

            if (empty($paymentDetailMetadata)) {
                $this->orderMeta->update($order, $paymentDetailKey, "[Date $date]");
            }
        }
    }
```

<wp_gen> Write a WordPress function:

<?php
/**
 * Retrieves all active subscriptions for a user within a date range.
 *
 * @param int    $user_id    User ID.
 * @param string $date_start Start date in Y-m-d format.
 * @param string $date_end   End date in Y-m-d format.
 * @return array<int, object> Array of subscription objects.
 */
function myplugin_get_user_subscriptions_209( int $user_id, string $date_start, string $date_end ): array {
	global $wpdb;
	$table = $wpdb->prefix . 'myplugin_subscriptions';

	$results = $wpdb->get_results(
		$wpdb->prepare(
			"SELECT id, plan_id, status, start_date, end_date
			 FROM {$table}
			 WHERE user_id = %d
			   AND start_date >= %s
			   AND end_date <= %s
			   AND status = %s
			 ORDER BY start_date DESC",
			$user_id,
			$date_start,
			$date_end,
			'active'
		)
	);

	return $results ?: [];
}

<wp_judge> Evaluate this WordPress code:

```php
function init_scripts() {
		$admin_pages = $this->admin_pages();
		
		if ( is_admin() && isset( $_REQUEST['page'] ) && in_array( $_REQUEST['page'], $admin_pages ) ) {
			add_action( 'admin_enqueue_scripts', array( $this, 'admin_script_load' ) );
			add_action( 'admin_enqueue_scripts', array( $this, 'register_modal_scripts' ), 0 );
			do_action( $this->plugin_name . '_init_scripts' );

			add_action( 'admin_print_scripts', array( $this, 'admin_localize_printed_scripts' ), 5 );
			add_action( 'admin_print_footer_scripts', array( $this, 'admin_localize_printed_scripts' ), 5 );
		}
	}
```

The method reads $_REQUEST['page'] without sanitization and passes it through in_array() against a whitelist, but does not apply sanitize_text_field() or sanitize_key() first. $_REQUEST merges GET, POST, and COOKIE, making the page parameter source ambiguous.

WPCS Compliance: severity medium (6/10) — Issue: Missing PHPDoc block entirely — no @since, @return void, or description. $_REQUEST is read without sanitization, and WPCS recommends using $_GET or $_POST explicitly instead of the ambiguous $_REQUEST.. Fix: Add a PHPDoc block with @since and @return void. Replace $_REQUEST with $_GET since admin page slugs are always passed via GET query string.

SQL Safety: severity low (8/10) — Issue: No database queries are performed in this script registration method; SQL safety does not apply.. Fix: No SQL changes required.

Security: severity high (4/10) — Issue: $_REQUEST['page'] is read and compared using in_array() without sanitization. While the in_array() whitelist check is a good first step, the unsanitized superglobal could interact with php://filter wrappers or multibyte characters in unexpected ways. Using $_REQUEST instead of $_GET also accepts POST and COOKIE values for a query-string parameter.. Fix: Replace $_REQUEST with $_GET and apply sanitize_key(wp_unslash($_GET['page'])) before the in_array() check to ensure the page slug is clean ASCII before comparison.

Performance: severity low (8/10) — Issue: do_action() is called inside the if block immediately during init_scripts(), firing the action before admin_enqueue_scripts has fired. This ordering may cause issues if hooked callbacks expect the script queue to be ready.. Fix: Consider moving do_action($this->plugin_name . '_init_scripts') inside an admin_enqueue_scripts callback rather than calling it directly in init_scripts().

WP API Usage: severity low (8/10) — Issue: is_admin() combined with a page slug check is the standard pattern for admin page detection. add_action() calls are correct.. Fix: No wp_api_usage changes required.

include_frontend_script() calls extract($slider_settings) on an array retrieved from post meta, allowing any key in the stored settings to silently overwrite local variables including $slider_template and potentially $our_shortcode and $post, creating a variable injection security risk.

WPCS Compliance: severity medium (6/10) — Issue: The function uses $_upload_dir with a leading underscore as a local variable name, which WPCS reserves for the indication of unused or private variables; local variables should not use the leading underscore convention.. Fix: Rename $_upload_dir to $upload_dir throughout the function body.

SQL Safety: severity low (8/10) — Issue: No direct SQL is executed; get_post() and get_post_meta() use the WP abstraction layer correctly.. Fix: No SQL changes needed.

Security: severity critical (2/10) — Issue: extract($slider_settings) unpacks database-stored post meta array keys as local variables without any allowlist; a malicious or corrupted meta value with key 'our_shortcode', 'slider_id', or 'post' would overwrite those variables silently, enabling variable injection from stored data.. Fix: Replace extract($slider_settings) with explicit key access: $slider_transition_effect = isset($slider_settings['slider_transition_effect']) ? $slider_settings['slider_transition_effect'] : ''; and access only the specific keys needed.

Performance: severity medium (6/10) — Issue: get_post() and get_post_meta() are called inside a foreach loop over shortcode matches; if a post has multiple a3_responsive_slider shortcodes with the same slider_id, the same post meta is fetched redundantly.. Fix: Cache $slider_data and $slider_settings results by $slider_id in a local array before the loop to avoid duplicate database calls for repeated slider IDs.

WP API Usage: severity low (8/10) — Issue: has_shortcode() followed by preg_match_all(get_shortcode_regex()) is the correct WP pattern for parsing shortcode attributes from post content.. Fix: No API changes needed; the shortcode parsing pattern is correct.

Code Quality: severity medium (6/10) — Issue: The condition ! empty( $matches ) && is_array( $matches ) && count( $matches ) > 0 contains redundant checks: ! empty() already implies is_array() returns true for a non-empty array, and count() > 0 is implied by ! empty().. Fix: Simplify to ! empty( $matches ) alone, removing the redundant is_array() and count() > 0 checks.

Dependency Integrity: severity low (8/10) — Issue: Relies on Functions::get_slider_transition() as a static call; this is an internal plugin dependency without version guarding but acceptable within a single plugin codebase.. Fix: No dependency changes needed.

i18n: severity low (8/10) — Issue: No user-facing strings are output by this function; it only registers scripts and styles.. Fix: No i18n changes needed.

Accessibility: severity low (8/10) — Issue: This function registers and enqueues scripts/styles; accessibility of the slider output depends on the frontend rendering, not this registration function.. Fix: No accessibility changes needed here.

Key observation: extract($slider_settings) on unvalidated post meta data is the critical issue; it allows any stored key to overwrite local variables including loop control variables, enabling variable injection from the database.

```php
/** @var _WP_Dependency $dependency */
function amend_twentytwentyone_styles() {
		add_action(
			'wp_enqueue_scripts',
			static function () {
				$style_handle = 'twenty-twenty-one-style';

				// Bail if the stylesheet is not enqueued.
				if ( ! wp_style_is( $style_handle ) ) {
					return; // @codeCoverageIgnore
				}

				$css_file = get_theme_file_path(
					sprintf( 'style%s.css', is_rtl() ? '-rtl' : '' )
				);

				if ( ! file_exists( $css_file ) ) {
					return; // @codeCoverageIgnore
				}

				
				$dependency = wp_styles()->registered[ $style_handle ];

				// Set the registered handle as an alias for other stylesheets to depend on.
				$dependency->src = false;

				$styles = file_get_contents( $css_file ); //phpcs:ignore WordPress.WP.AlternativeFunctions.file_get_contents_file_get_contents

				// AMP provides a global API "AMP.toggleTheme()" to support dark theme by toggling a BODY element class.
				// However, for dark mode in the Twenty Twenty-One theme, the class needs to be toggled on HTML and BODY elements because the CSS variables are set at the root level.
				// In AMP we cannot toggle HTML classes. So here we are changing the location where CSS variables are defined from `:root` to the BODY element.
				if ( get_theme_mod( 'respect_user_color_preference', false ) ) {
					$styles = str_replace( ':root {', 'body {', $styles );
				}

				// Append extra rules needed for nav menus according to changes made to the document during sanitization.
				$styles .= '
					/* Trap keyboard navigation within mobile menu when it\'s open */
					@media only screen and (max-width: 481px) {
						.primary-navigation-open #page {
							visibility: hidden;
						}

						.primary-navigation-open .menu-button-container {
							visibility: visible;
						}
					}

					@media (min-width: 482px) {
						/* Show the sub-menu on hover of menu item */
						.primary-menu-container > .menu-wrapper > .menu-item-has-children:hover > .sub-menu {
							display: block;
						}

						/* Hide the plus icon on hover of menu item */
						.primary-menu-container > .menu-wrapper > .menu-item-has-children:hover > .sub-menu-toggle > .icon-plus {
							display: none;
						}

						/* Show the minus icon on hover of menu item */
						.primary-menu-container > .menu-wrapper > .menu-item-has-children:hover > .sub-menu-toggle > .icon-minus {
							display: flex;
						}
					}
				';

				/*
				 * In Twenty Twenty-One, when a button is used to resize AMP elements, they can appear transparent when hovered over.
				 * To resolve this issue, the theme's background color is used as the background color for the button
				 * when it is in the hovered state.
				 */
				$styles .= '
					button[overflow]:hover {
						background-color: var(--global--color-background);
					}
				';

				// Ideally wp_add_inline_style() would accept a $position argument like wp_add_inline_script() does, in
				// which case the following could be replaced with wp_add_inline_style( $style_handle, $new_styles, 'before' ).
				// But this is not supported, so we have to resort to manipulating the underlying after array.
				if ( ! isset( $dependency->extra['after'] ) ) {
					$dependency->extra['after'] = [];
				}
				array_unshift( $dependency->extra['after'], $styles );
			},
			11
		);
	}
```

WPCS Compliance: score 7/10 — Uses wp_style_is(), get_theme_file_path(), get_theme_mod() correctly per WPCS; phpcs:ignore comment present for file_get_contents; RTL handled with is_rtl(); outer function lacks docblock @param/@return tags.

SQL Safety: score None/10 — No $wpdb usage or SQL queries present; CSS file manipulation only.

Security: score 7/10 — CSS file path is constructed via get_theme_file_path() (WP core function) preventing path traversal; file_get_contents is constrained to a local resolved theme file path; $styles output goes through WP's style injection mechanism, not direct echo.

Performance: score 6/10 — file_get_contents() reads a full CSS file on every wp_enqueue_scripts action invocation; no caching of the file contents means repeated filesystem reads per page load, which is a performance concern at scale.

Code Quality: score 8/10 — Thorough inline comments explaining AMP constraints, RTL handling, and the wp_add_inline_style limitation workaround; defensive isset() guard before array_unshift; static closure correctly used.

Dependency Integrity: score 7/10 — Depends on WP core style registration internals (wp_styles()->registered array structure) which could break on WP core changes; this is acknowledged as a workaround in comments.

Any unescaped echo in an HTML attribute or element context is an XSS vector.. Duplicate keys mean only the last value takes effect, silently discarding the first definition.. Fix: Remove duplicate keys from $default_atts and resolve or remove the @TODO comment before shipping.

i18n: severity low (8/10) — Issue: Term names from $term->name are taxonomy data stored in the database and already in the site language; wrapping them with __() is incorrect. No hardcoded English strings are echoed directly.. Fix: No i18n changes required.

```php
			} else {

		}

		$woo_builder_enabled = get_post_meta( get_the_ID(), '_themeisle_gutenberg_woo_builder', true );
```

```php
/**
	 * Evaluate dynamic media content
	 *
	 * @param string           $path Current image path.
	 * @param \WP_REST_Request $request Request data.
	 *
	 * @since 2.0.9
	 * @return string
	 */
function evaluate_content_media_server( $path, $request ) {
		$type    = $request->get_param( 'type' );
		$context = $request->get_param( 'context' );
		$id      = $request->get_param( 'id' );
		$meta    = $request->get_param( 'meta' );

		if ( 'postMeta' === $type && ! empty( $meta ) ) {
			$value = get_post_meta( $context, $meta, true );

			if ( ! empty( $value ) ) {
				$path = esc_url( $value );
			}
		}

		if ( 'product' === $type && class_exists( 'WooCommerce' ) && ! empty( $id ) ) {
			$product = wc_get_product( $id );
			$image   = $product->get_image_id();

			if ( $image ) {
				$path = wp_get_original_image_path( intval( $image ) );
			} else {
				$image = get_option( 'woocommerce_placeholder_image', 0 );

				if ( $image ) {
					$path = wp_get_original_image_path( $image );
				}
			}
		}

		if ( 'acf' === $type && ! empty( $meta ) && class_exists( 'ACF' ) ) {
			$field = get_field( $meta, $context );

			if ( ! empty( $field ) ) {
				if ( is_array( $field ) && isset( $field['ID'] ) ) {
					$path = wp_get_original_image_path( $field['ID'] );
				}

				if ( is_string( $field ) ) {
					$image = $this->get_image_id_from_url( $field );

					if ( $image ) {
						$path = wp_get_original_image_path( $image );
					} else {
						$path = $field;
					}
				}

				if ( is_int( $field ) ) {
					$path = wp_get_original_image_path( $field );
				}
			}
		}

		return $path;
	}
```

```php
function is_writable() {
		global $wp_filesystem;
		include_once ABSPATH . 'wp-admin/includes/file.php';
		WP_Filesystem();

		$wp_upload_dir = wp_upload_dir( null, false );
		$upload_dir    = $wp_upload_dir['basedir'];

		if ( ! function_exists( 'WP_Filesystem' ) ) {
```

```php
/**
	 * Forward the prompt to OpenAI API.
	 *
	 * @param \WP_REST_Request $request Request object.
	 * @return \WP_Error|\WP_HTTP_Response|\WP_REST_Response
	 */
function forward_prompt( $request ) {
		// Get the body from request and decode it.
		$body = $request->get_body();
		$body = json_decode( $body, true );

		$api_key = get_option( 'themeisle_open_ai_api_key' );

		// Extract the data from keys that start with 'otter_'.
		$otter_data = array_filter(
			$body,
			function ( $key ) {
				return 0 === strpos( $key, 'otter_' );
			},
			ARRAY_FILTER_USE_KEY
		);

		// Remove the values which keys start with 'otter_'.
		$body = array_diff_key( $body, $otter_data );

		$response = wp_remote_post(
			self::BASE_URL,
			array(
				'method'  => 'POST',
				'headers' => array(
					'Authorization' => 'Bearer ' . $api_key,
					'Content-Type'  => 'application/json',
				),
				'body'    => wp_json_encode( $body ),
				'timeout' => 2 * MINUTE_IN_SECONDS,
			)
		);

		if ( is_wp_error( $response ) ) {
			return $response;
		}

		$body = wp_remote_retrieve_body( $response );
		$body = json_decode( $body, true );

		if ( json_last_error() !== JSON_ERROR_NONE ) {
			return new \WP_Error( 'rest_invalid_json', __( 'Could not parse the response from OpenAI. Try again.', 'otter-blocks' ), array( 'status' => 400 ) );
		}

		return new \WP_REST_Response( $body, wp_remote_retrieve_response_code( $response ) );
	}
```

forward_prompt passes user-supplied request body to an external API with no capability check and no validation that the stored API key is non-empty, allowing unauthenticated callers to consume the site's OpenAI key if the REST route's permission_callback is ever misconfigured.

WPCS Compliance: severity low (8/10) — Issue: forward_prompt has no WPCS-specific violations beyond the missing capability check; code style is otherwise compliant.. Fix: No WPCS-specific style changes required.

SQL Safety: severity low (8/10) — Issue: forward_prompt performs no database queries; SQL safety is not applicable.. Fix: No SQL changes required.

Security: severity critical (2/10) — Issue: forward_prompt does not call current_user_can() before forwarding the request, and $api_key is sent even when empty (sending 'Bearer ' with no token); any misconfigured permission_callback on the REST route exposes the API key to unauthenticated use.. Fix: Add if ( ! current_user_can( 'edit_posts' ) ) { return new \WP_Error( 'rest_forbidden', '', array( 'status' => 403 ) ); } at the start of forward_prompt, and guard with if ( empty( $api_key ) ) before calling wp_remote_post().

Performance: severity low (8/10) — Issue: forward_prompt correctly sets a 2-minute timeout for the wp_remote_post() call to OpenAI; no performance concern.. Fix: No performance changes required.

WP API Usage: severity low (8/10) — Issue: forward_prompt correctly uses wp_remote_post(), wp_remote_retrieve_body(), wp_remote_retrieve_response_code(), and wp_json_encode() from the WordPress HTTP API.. Fix: No wp_api_usage changes required.

Code Quality: severity medium (6/10) — Issue: forward_prompt reuses the variable name $body for both the request body and the decoded response body, making the function harder to read and maintain.. Fix: Use separate variable names: $request_body for the incoming decoded JSON and $response_body for the decoded OpenAI response.

Dependency Integrity: severity low (8/10) — Issue: forward_prompt depends on the 'themeisle_open_ai_api_key' option being set, which is documented in the plugin's settings; no structural dependency concern.. Fix: Add an empty check on $api_key to surface misconfiguration as an error rather than sending an invalid Authorization header.

i18n: severity low (8/10) — Issue: forward_prompt correctly wraps the error string with __() using the 'otter-blocks' text domain.. Fix: No i18n changes required.

Accessibility: severity low (8/10) — Issue: forward_prompt is a REST API handler with no HTML output; accessibility is not applicable.. Fix: No accessibility changes required.

Key observation: The critical vulnerability is absent capability enforcement inside the function body — a REST permission_callback is the first line of defense but the handler must also call current_user_can() defensively, and an empty API key must be caught before it is forwarded.

```php
	 * This method will pe passed to the render_callback parameter and it will output
```

```php
/**
	 * Enqueue FSE CSS file
	 *
	 * @since   2.0.10
	 * @access  public
	 */
function enqueue_fse_css() {
		if ( ! ( function_exists( 'get_block_templates' ) && ( current_theme_supports( 'block-templates' ) || current_theme_supports( 'block-template-parts' ) ) ) ) {
			return;
		}

		global $_wp_current_template_content;

		$content = '';
		$slugs   = array();

		// If we have template content (full block templates), extract template part slugs.
		if ( ! empty( $_wp_current_template_content ) ) {
			$template_blocks = parse_blocks( $_wp_current_template_content );
			
			foreach ( $template_blocks as $template_block ) {
				if ( 'core/template-part' === $template_block['blockName'] && isset( $template_block['attrs']['slug'] ) ) {
					$slugs[] = $template_block['attrs']['slug'];
				}
			}
			
			// Get the specific template parts referenced in the template.
			$templates_parts = get_block_templates( array( 'slug__in' => $slugs ), 'wp_template_part' );
			
			foreach ( $templates_parts as $templates_part ) {
				if ( ! empty( $templates_part->content ) && ! empty( $templates_part->slug ) && in_array( $templates_part->slug, $slugs ) ) {
					$content .= $templates_part->content;
				}
			}
			
			$content .= $_wp_current_template_content;
		} else {
			// Fallback for classic themes with block-template-parts only.
			// Get all template parts since we can't determine which ones are used.
			$templates_parts = get_block_templates( array(), 'wp_template_part' );
			
			foreach ( $templates_parts as $templates_part ) {
				if ( ! empty( $templates_part->content ) ) {
					$content .= $templates_part->content;
				}
			}
		}

		$blocks = parse_blocks( $content );

		if ( ! is_array( $blocks ) || empty( $blocks ) ) {
			return '';
		}

		$animations = boolval( preg_match( '/\banimated\b/', $content ) );

		$css = $this->cycle_through_blocks( $blocks, $animations );

		if ( empty( $css ) ) {
			return;
		}

		if ( count( self::$google_fonts ) > 0 ) {
			$fonts = $this->get_fonts( self::$google_fonts );

			if ( count( $fonts['fonts'] ) > 0 ) {
				wp_enqueue_style( 'otter-google-fonts', $fonts['url'], [], null ); // phpcs:ignore WordPress.WP.EnqueuedResourceParameters.MissingVersion
			}
		}

		$style  = "\n" . '<style type="text/css" media="all">' . "\n";
		$style .= $css;
		$style .= "\n" . '</style>' . "\n";

		echo $style;// phpcs:ignore WordPress.Security.EscapeOutput.OutputNotEscaped
	}
```

Function `enqueue_fse_css` directly echoes a `<style>` block containing CSS derived from block template content without sanitization, instead of using `wp_add_inline_style()`, and has inconsistent return types (empty string vs. void) across branches.

Fix: Add `@return void` to the PHPDoc of `enqueue_fse_css` and change `in_array( $templates_part->slug, $slugs )` to `in_array( $templates_part->slug, $slugs, true )`.

Fix: In `enqueue_fse_css`, add a `'number' => 50` limit to the fallback `get_block_templates()` call, or cache the result in a transient keyed to the active theme slug.

WP API Usage: severity high (4/10) — Issue: Function `enqueue_fse_css` is named `enqueue_fse_css` but does not enqueue CSS via WordPress APIs — it directly echoes a `<style>` block, bypassing `wp_add_inline_style()` which is the correct WordPress API for adding inline CSS to the page.. function enqueue_styles( $post_id = null ) {
```php
		$post_id = $post_id ? $post_id : get_the_ID();

		if ( ! function_exists( 'has_blocks' ) ) {
			return;
		}

		if ( ! has_blocks( $post_id ) ) {
			return;
		}

		if ( is_preview() ) {
			add_action(
				'wp_footer',
				function () use ( $post_id ) {
					return $this->get_post_css( $post_id );
				},
				'the_content' === current_filter() ? PHP_INT_MAX : 10
			);
			return;
		}

		if ( ! CSS_Handler::has_css_file( $post_id ) ) {
			if ( CSS_Handler::is_writable() ) {
				CSS_Handler::generate_css_file( $post_id );
			}
			add_action(
				'wp_footer',
				function () use ( $post_id ) {
					return $this->get_post_css( $post_id );
				},
				'the_content' === current_filter() ? PHP_INT_MAX : 10
			);
			return;
		}

		$file_url = CSS_Handler::get_css_url( $post_id );
		$file_name = basename( $file_url );
		$content = get_post_field( 'post_content', $post_id );
		$blocks = parse_blocks( $content );

		if ( is_array( $blocks ) ) {
			$this->enqueue_reusable_styles( $blocks );
		}

		$total_inline_limit = 20000;
		$total_inline_limit = apply_filters( 'styles_inline_size_limit', 20000 );

		$wp_upload_dir = wp_upload_dir( null, false );
		$basedir       = $wp_upload_dir['basedir'] . '/themeisle-gutenberg/';
		$file_path     = $basedir . $file_name;
		$file_size     = filesize( $file_path );

		if ( $this->total_inline_size + $file_size < $total_inline_limit ) {
			add_action(
				'wp_footer',
				function () use ( $post_id ) {
					return $this->get_post_css( $post_id );
				},
				'the_content' === current_filter() ? PHP_INT_MAX : 10
			);
			$this->total_inline_size += (int) $file_size;
			return;
		}

		if ( 'the_content' === current_filter() ) {
			wp_enqueue_style( 'otter-' . $file_name, $file_url, array(), OTTER_BLOCKS_VERSION );
			return;
		}

		add_action(
			'wp_footer',
			function () use ( $file_name, $file_url ) {
				wp_enqueue_style( 'otter-' . $file_name, $file_url, array(), OTTER_BLOCKS_VERSION );
			}
		);
	}
```

enqueue_styles() assigns $total_inline_limit = 20000 immediately overwritten by apply_filters() making the first assignment dead code; calls filesize() on an unverified path risking PHP warnings and corrupt arithmetic; and closures passed to add_action() use return statements whose values are silently discarded by the hook system.

SQL Safety: severity low (8/10) — Issue: No direct SQL; get_post_field() and related calls use WP abstraction.. Fix: No SQL changes needed.

Security: severity medium (6/10) — Issue: $file_url from CSS_Handler::get_css_url($post_id) is passed to wp_enqueue_style() without esc_url(); even though wp_enqueue_style escapes internally, explicitly calling esc_url() on the URL before use is required by WPCS for any URL derived from dynamic sources.. Fix: Wrap the URL: $file_url = esc_url( CSS_Handler::get_css_url( $post_id ) ); before it is passed to wp_enqueue_style().

```php
/**
	 * Apply the 'after_submit' action when changing the status from 'draft' to 'unread'.
	 *
	 * @param WP_Post $post The post.
	 */
function apply_hooks_on_draft_transition( $post ) {
		if ( self::FORM_RECORD_TYPE !== $post->post_type ) {
			return;
		}

		$meta = get_post_meta( $post->ID, self::FORM_RECORD_META_KEY, true );

		if ( ! isset( $meta['dump'] ) || empty( $meta['dump']['value'] ) ) {
			return;
		}

		$form_data = Form_Data_Request::create_from_dump( $meta['dump']['value'] );
		$form_data->mark_as_duplicate();
		$form_options = Form_Settings_Data::get_form_setting_from_wordpress_options( $form_data->get_data_from_payload( 'formOption' ) );
		$form_data->set_form_options( $form_options );
		$form_data = Form_Server::pull_fields_options_for_form( $form_data );

		do_action( 'otter_form_on_submission_confirmed', $form_data );

		if (
			! isset( $form_data ) ||
			( ! class_exists( 'ThemeIsle\GutenbergBlocks\Integration\Form_Data_Request' ) ) ||
			! ( $form_data instanceof \ThemeIsle\GutenbergBlocks\Integration\Form_Data_Request )
		) {
			return;
		}

		do_action( 'otter_form_after_submit', $form_data );
	}
```

WPCS Compliance: score 7/10 — Indentation and spacing follow WordPress standards; Yoda condition used correctly for post_type check. Minor: $post parameter lacks type hint in the function signature despite docblock declaring WP_Post.

SQL Safety: score None/10 — No $wpdb or raw SQL; get_post_meta uses WordPress core API exclusively.

Security: score 4/10 — No capability check before processing the post transition; any status change on a FORM_RECORD_TYPE post triggers form re-submission actions including do_action('otter_form_after_submit'), which could send emails or write data without verifying the initiating user has permission. The null-check on $form_data after do_action('otter_form_on_submission_confirmed') is logically unreachable since $form_data was set prior.

Performance: score 6/10 — get_post_meta is called with $single=true which is correct; Form_Server::pull_fields_options_for_form and Form_Settings_Data::get_form_setting_from_wordpress_options may trigger additional DB reads but are not cached here.

WP API Usage: score 7/10 — get_post_meta and do_action are used correctly. class_exists check before instanceof is a good defensive pattern, though the check occurs after do_action('otter_form_on_submission_confirmed') has already fired, making the guard too late.

Code Quality: score 5/10 — Post-action null-guard on $form_data is dead code since $form_data is assigned before the first do_action and cannot become null through that call. Logic ordering defect: otter_form_on_submission_confirmed fires before the instanceof validation, meaning the action receives potentially invalid data.

Dependency Integrity: score 6/10 — Depends on Form_Data_Request, Form_Settings_Data, Form_Server — all plugin-internal classes. class_exists guard is present but only evaluated after the first action fires, leaving a partial dependency failure undetected.

i18n: score 2/10 — No __() or _e() calls present; all string literals are WordPress action hook names ('otter_form_on_submission_confirmed', 'otter_form_after_submit') and array keys, none of which require translation.

```php
function sanitize_shop_ids_field( $input ) {
		$values = isset( $_POST['shop_ids'] ) ? $_POST['shop_ids'] : [];

		$shop_ids = [];
		foreach ( $values as $value ) {
			$value = trim( $value );
			if ( $value ) {
				$shop_ids[] = $value;

				$transaction = new \Payone\Transaction\RatepayProfile( $this );
				$response    = $transaction->execute( $value );

				if ( $response->get( 'status' ) === 'OK' ) {
					$shop_id_data = [
						'currency'              => $response->get( 'add_paydata[currency]' ),
						'country_code_billing'  => $response->get( 'add_paydata[country-code-billing]' ),
						'country_code_delivery' => $response->get( 'add_paydata[country-code-delivery]' ),
						'invoice_sum_min'       => $response->get( 'add_paydata[tx-limit-invoice-min]' ),
						'invoice_sum_max'       => $response->get( 'add_paydata[tx-limit-invoice-max]' ),
						'direct_debit_sum_min'  => $response->get( 'add_paydata[tx-limit-elv-min]' ),
						'direct_debit_sum_max'  => $response->get( 'add_paydata[tx-limit-elv-max]' ),
						'month_allowed'         => $response->get( 'add_paydata[month-allowed]' ),
						'installments_sum_min'  => $response->get( 'add_paydata[tx-limit-installment-min]' ),
						'installments_sum_max'  => $response->get( 'add_paydata[tx-limit-installment-max]' ),
					];

					$shop_ids_data           = (array) $this->get_option( 'shop_ids_data', [] );
					$shop_ids_data[ $value ] = $shop_id_data;
					$this->update_option( 'shop_ids_data', $shop_ids_data );
				}
			}
		}

		return implode( ',', $shop_ids );
	}
```

A WooCommerce settings sanitization callback reads shop IDs from $_POST and calls update_option() to store external API response data without any nonce verification, enabling CSRF-driven option manipulation.

Fix: Add check_admin_referer( 'woocommerce-settings' ) or verify wp_verify_nonce( sanitize_key( wp_unslash( $_POST['_wpnonce'] ) ), 'woocommerce-settings' ) before reading $_POST['shop_ids'].

```php
/**
		 * Filter the CMB2 Field arguments after processing.
		 *
		 * @since 2.6.0
		 * @param array             $args  Metabox field config array after processing.
		 * @param CMB2_Field object $field This field object.
		 */
function _set_field_defaults( $args ) {
		$defaults = $this->get_default_field_args( $args );

		
		$defaults = apply_filters( 'cmb2_field_defaults', $defaults, $args['id'], $args['type'], $this );

		// Set up blank or default values for empty ones.
		$args = wp_parse_args( $args, $defaults );

		
		$args = apply_filters( 'cmb2_field_arguments_raw', $args, $this );

		/*
		 * Deprecated usage:
		 *
		 * 'std' -- use 'default' (no longer works)
		 * 'row_classes' -- use 'class', or 'class_cb'
		 * 'default' -- as callback (use default_cb)
		 */
		$args = $this->convert_deprecated_params( $args );

		$args['repeatable'] = $args['repeatable'] && ! $this->repeatable_exception( $args['type'] );
		$args['inline']     = $args['inline'] || false !== stripos( $args['type'], '_inline' );
		$args['_id']        = $args['id'];
		$args['_name']      = $args['id'];

		if ( $method = $this->has_args_method( $args['type'] ) ) {
			$args = $this->{$method}( $args );
		}

		if ( $this->group ) {
			$args = $this->set_group_sub_field_defaults( $args );
		}

		$with_supporting = array(
			// CMB2_Sanitize::_save_file_id_value()/CMB2_Sanitize::_get_group_file_value_array().
			'file' => '_id',
			// See CMB2_Sanitize::_save_utc_value().
			'text_datetime_timestamp_timezone' => '_utc',
		);

		$args['has_supporting_data'] = isset( $with_supporting[ $args['type'] ] )
			? $with_supporting[ $args['type'] ]
			: false;

		// Repeatable fields require jQuery sortable library.
		if ( ! empty( $args['repeatable'] ) ) {
			CMB2_JS::add_dependencies( 'jquery-ui-sortable' );
		}

		
		return apply_filters( 'cmb2_field_arguments', $args, $this );
	}
```

```php
function init() {

		// Prepare domains info.
		$return           = OBJECT;
		$current_language = pll_current_language( $return );
		$default_language = pll_default_language( $return );

		if ( ! $current_language || ! $default_language ) {
			return;
		}

		$is_preview = ! empty( sanitize_key( wp_unslash( $_GET['elementor_preview'] ) ) );

		if ( ! cpel_is_elementor_editor() && ! $is_preview ) {
			return;
		}

		$domains = pll_languages_list( array( 'fields' => 'home_url' ) );

		foreach ( $domains as $domain ) {
			$this->all_domains[] = wp_parse_url( $domain, PHP_URL_HOST );
		}

		if ( method_exists( $current_language, 'get_home_url' ) ) {
			$this->current_domain = $current_language->get_home_url();
			$this->default_domain = $default_language->get_home_url();
		} else {
			$this->current_domain = $current_language->home_url;
			$this->default_domain = $default_language->home_url;
		}

		// Add filters.
		add_filter( 'script_loader_src', array( $this, 'translate_url' ) );
		add_filter( 'style_loader_src', array( $this, 'translate_url' ) );

		add_filter( 'allowed_http_origins', array( $this, 'add_allowed_origins' ) );

		add_filter( 'admin_url', array( $this, 'replace_ajax_url' ), 10, 3 );
		add_filter( 'wp_get_attachment_image_attributes', array( $this, 'replace_src' ), 10, 2 );

		add_filter( 'elementor/editor/localize_settings', array( $this, 'translate_url_recursive' ) );

	}
```

```php
/**
	 * Check whether a given request has permission to show plugin version.
	 *
	 * @author George Gecewicz <george.gecewicz@webdevstudios.com>
	 * @since  1.2.0
	 *
	 * @param  WP_REST_Request $request Full details about the request.
	 * @return WP_Error|boolean
	 */
function get_item_permissions_check( $request ) {
		if ( ! wc_rest_check_manager_permissions( 'settings', 'read' ) ) {

			$ctct_logger = new DebugLogging(
				wc_get_logger(),
				'CTCT Woo: no permission to check version',
				'warning',
				[ 'cc-woo-rest-not-allowed' => $request ]
			);
			$ctct_logger->log();

			return new WP_Error( 'cc-woo-rest-not-allowed', esc_html__( 'Sorry, you cannot list that resource.', 'constant-contact-woocommerce' ), [ 'status' => rest_authorization_required_code() ] );
		}

		return true;
	}
```

```php
	 * Fires after a post type is updated to our saved options.
	 *
	 * @since 1.0.0
	 *
	 * @param array $data Array of post type data that was updated.
	 */
function cptui_update_post_type( $data = [] ) {

	
	do_action( 'cptui_before_update_post_type', $data );

	// They need to provide a name.
	if ( empty( $data['cpt_custom_post_type']['name'] ) ) {
		return cptui_admin_notices( 'error', '', false, esc_html__( 'Please provide a post type name', 'custom-post-type-ui' ) );
	}

	if ( ! empty( $data['cpt_original'] ) && $data['cpt_original'] != $data['cpt_custom_post_type']['name'] ) { // phpcs:ignore.
		if ( ! empty( $data['update_post_types'] ) ) {
			add_filter( 'cptui_convert_post_type_posts', '__return_true' );
		}
	}

	// Clean up $_POST data.
	foreach ( $data as $key => $value ) {
		if ( is_string( $value ) ) {
			$data[ $key ] = sanitize_text_field( $value );
		} else {
			array_map( 'sanitize_text_field', $data[ $key ] );
		}
	}

	// Check if they didn't put quotes in the name or rewrite slug.
	if ( false !== strpos( $data['cpt_custom_post_type']['name'], '\'' ) ||
		false !== strpos( $data['cpt_custom_post_type']['name'], '\"' ) ||
		false !== strpos( $data['cpt_custom_post_type']['rewrite_slug'], '\'' ) ||
		false !== strpos( $data['cpt_custom_post_type']['rewrite_slug'], '\"' ) ) {

		add_filter( 'cptui_custom_error_message', 'cptui_slug_has_quotes' );
		return 'error';
	}

	$post_types = cptui_get_post_type_data();

	
	$slug_exists = apply_filters( 'cptui_post_type_slug_exists', false, $data['cpt_custom_post_type']['name'], $post_types );
	if ( true === $slug_exists ) {
		add_filter( 'cptui_custom_error_message', 'cptui_slug_matches_post_type' );
		return 'error';
	}
	if ( 'new' === $data['cpt_type_status'] ) {
		$slug_as_page = cptui_check_page_slugs( $data['cpt_custom_post_type']['name'] );
		if ( true === $slug_as_page ) {
			add_filter( 'cptui_custom_error_message', 'cptui_slug_matches_page' );
			return 'error';
		}
	}

	if ( empty( $data['cpt_addon_taxes'] ) || ! is_array( $data['cpt_addon_taxes'] ) ) {
		$data['cpt_addon_taxes'] = [];
	}

	if ( empty( $data['cpt_supports'] ) || ! is_array( $data['cpt_supports'] ) ) {
		$data['cpt_supports'] = [];
	}

	if ( empty( $data['cpt_labels'] ) || ! is_array( $data['cpt_labels'] ) ) {
		$data['cpt_labels'] = [];
	}

	foreach ( $data['cpt_labels'] as $key => $label ) {
		if ( empty( $label ) ) {
			unset( $data['cpt_labels'][ $key ] );
		}

		$label = str_replace( '"', '', htmlspecialchars_decode( $label ) );
		$label = htmlspecialchars( $label, ENT_QUOTES );
		$label = trim( $label );
		if ( 'parent' === $key ) {
			$data['cpt_labels']['parent_item_colon'] = stripslashes_deep( $label );
		} else {
			$data['cpt_labels'][ $key ] = stripslashes_deep( $label );
		}
	}

	$menu_icon = trim( $data['cpt_custom_post_type']['menu_icon'] );
	if ( '' === $data['cpt_custom_post_type']['menu_icon'] ) {
		$menu_icon = null;
	}

	$register_meta_box_cb = trim( $data['cpt_custom_post_type']['register_meta_box_cb'] );
	if ( empty( $register_meta_box_cb ) ) {
		$register_meta_box_cb = null;
	}

	$label = ucwords( str_replace( '_', ' ', $data['cpt_custom_post_type']['name'] ) );
	if ( ! empty( $data['cpt_custom_post_type']['label'] ) ) {
		$label = str_replace( '"', '', htmlspecialchars_decode( $data['cpt_custom_post_type']['label'] ) );
		$label = htmlspecialchars( stripslashes( $label ), ENT_QUOTES );
	}

	$singular_label = ucwords( str_replace( '_', ' ', $data['cpt_custom_post_type']['name'] ) );
	if ( ! empty( $data['cpt_custom_post_type']['singular_label'] ) ) {
		$singular_label = str_replace( '"', '', htmlspecialchars_decode( $data['cpt_custom_post_type']['singular_label'] ) );
		$singular_label = htmlspecialchars( stripslashes( $singular_label ), ENT_QUOTES );
	}

	$description = cptui_get_saved_description();

	$name                  = trim( $data['cpt_custom_post_type']['name'] );
	$rest_base             = trim( $data['cpt_custom_post_type']['rest_base'] );
	$rest_controller_class = trim( $data['cpt_custom_post_type']['rest_controller_class'] );
	$rest_namespace        = trim( $data['cpt_custom_post_type']['rest_namespace'] );
	$has_archive_string    = trim( $data['cpt_custom_post_type']['has_archive_string'] );
	$capability_type       = trim( $data['cpt_custom_post_type']['capability_type'] );
	$rewrite_slug          = trim( $data['cpt_custom_post_type']['rewrite_slug'] );
	$query_var_slug        = trim( $data['cpt_custom_post_type']['query_var_slug'] );
	$menu_position         = trim( $data['cpt_custom_post_type']['menu_position'] );
	$show_in_menu_string   = trim( $data['cpt_custom_post_type']['show_in_menu_string'] );
	$custom_supports       = trim( $data['cpt_custom_post_type']['custom_supports'] );
	$enter_title_here      = trim( $data['cpt_custom_post_type']['enter_title_here'] );

	$post_types[ $data['cpt_custom_post_type']['name'] ] = [
		'name'                  => $name,
		'label'                 => $label,
		'singular_label'        => $singular_label,
		'description'           => $description,
		'public'                => disp_boolean( $data['cpt_custom_post_type']['public'] ),
		'publicly_queryable'    => disp_boolean( $data['cpt_custom_post_type']['publicly_queryable'] ),
		'show_ui'               => disp_boolean( $data['cpt_custom_post_type']['show_ui'] ),
		'show_in_nav_menus'     => disp_boolean( $data['cpt_custom_post_type']['show_in_nav_menus'] ),
		'delete_with_user'      => disp_boolean( $data['cpt_custom_post_type']['delete_with_user'] ),
		'show_in_rest'          => disp_boolean( $data['cpt_custom_post_type']['show_in_rest'] ),
		'rest_base'             => $rest_base,
		'rest_controller_class' => $rest_controller_class,
		'rest_namespace'        => $rest_namespace,
		'has_archive'           => disp_boolean( $data['cpt_custom_post_type']['has_archive'] ),
		'has_archive_string'    => $has_archive_string,
		'exclude_from_search'   => disp_boolean( $data['cpt_custom_post_type']['exclude_from_search'] ),
		'capability_type'       => $capability_type,
		'hierarchical'          => disp_boolean( $data['cpt_custom_post_type']['hierarchical'] ),
		'can_export'            => disp_boolean( $data['cpt_custom_post_type']['can_export'] ),
		'rewrite'               => disp_boolean( $data['cpt_custom_post_type']['rewrite'] ),
		'rewrite_slug'          => $rewrite_slug,
		'rewrite_withfront'     => disp_boolean( $data['cpt_custom_post_type']['rewrite_withfront'] ),
		'query_var'             => disp_boolean( $data['cpt_custom_post_type']['query_var'] ),
		'query_var_slug'        => $query_var_slug,
		'menu_position'         => $menu_position,
		'show_in_menu'          => disp_boolean( $data['cpt_custom_post_type']['show_in_menu'] ),
		'show_in_menu_string'   => $show_in_menu_string,
		'menu_icon'             => $menu_icon,
		'register_meta_box_cb'  => $register_meta_box_cb,
		'supports'              => $data['cpt_supports'],
		'taxonomies'            => $data['cpt_addon_taxes'],
		'labels'                => $data['cpt_labels'],
		'custom_supports'       => $custom_supports,
		'enter_title_here'      => $enter_title_here,
	];

	
	$post_types = apply_filters( 'cptui_pre_save_post_type', $post_types, $name );

	
	if ( false === ( $success = apply_filters( 'cptui_post_type_update_save', false, $post_types, $data ) ) ) { // phpcs:ignore.
		$success = update_option( 'cptui_post_types', $post_types );
	}

	
	do_action( 'cptui_after_update_post_type', $data );

	// Used to help flush rewrite rules on init.
	set_transient( 'cptui_flush_rewrite_rules', 'true', 5 * 60 );

	if ( isset( $success ) && 'new' === $data['cpt_type_status'] ) {
		return 'add_success';
	}
	return 'update_success';
}
```

The function processes post type save operations from $data (which originates from $_POST) without any nonce verification, and the array_map sanitization call is broken — it discards its return value instead of assigning it back.

WPCS Compliance: severity medium (6/10) — Issue: PHPDoc describes a hook fired after update but this is the function that performs the update, not a hook callback; the description is misleading. Extra blank lines throughout violate WPCS blank line limits.. Fix: Correct PHPDoc to 'Updates a post type configuration and saves to the cptui_post_types option.' Remove extra blank lines.

SQL Safety: severity low (8/10) — Issue: No raw SQL in cptui_update_post_type; uses update_option() for all persistence.. Fix: No SQL changes needed.

Security: severity critical (2/10) — Issue: cptui_update_post_type processes $data which originates from $_POST form submission with no nonce check visible in the function; a CSRF attack could register arbitrary custom post types on the site.. Fix: Add check_admin_referer( 'cptui_addedit_post_type_nonce', 'cptui_addedit_post_type_nonce' ) at the top of the function before processing $data.

Performance: severity low (8/10) — Issue: Multiple trim() and htmlspecialchars() calls on each label during save; acceptable one-time cost on settings save.. Fix: No performance changes needed.

WP API Usage: severity low (8/10) — Issue: Uses apply_filters, do_action, update_option, set_transient, cptui_admin_notices correctly per WordPress API conventions.. Fix: No wp_api_usage changes needed beyond the nonce check.

Code Quality: severity high (4/10) — Issue: The array_map( 'sanitize_text_field', $data[ $key ] ) call in the sanitization foreach discards its return value; array_map() does not modify in place, so non-string values in $data are never actually sanitized.. Fix: Change to $data[ $key ] = array_map( 'sanitize_text_field', (array) $data[ $key ] ) to capture the sanitized result.

Dependency Integrity: severity low (8/10) — Issue: Depends on cptui_get_post_type_data, cptui_admin_notices, cptui_check_page_slugs, cptui_get_saved_description, disp_boolean — all plugin-internal functions expected to be available.. Fix: No dependency changes needed.

i18n: severity low (8/10) — Issue: User-facing error message uses esc_html__() with 'custom-post-type-ui' text domain correctly; no i18n issues.. Fix: No i18n changes needed.

Accessibility: severity low (8/10) — Issue: No direct HTML output; returns status strings for calling code to handle display.. Fix: No accessibility changes needed.

Key observation: Two compounding bugs: missing nonce check enables CSRF-driven post type registration, and the broken array_map call means nested array data in $data is never actually sanitized.

[/REASONING]

```php
/**
	 * Enqueues the necessary Elementor script for the current post.
	 *
	 * @return void
	 */
function enqueue_elementor_script() {
		$post = \get_post();

		if ( ! $post instanceof WP_Post ) {
			return;
		}

		$edit_js_object = $this->generate_js_object( $post );
		$this->asset_manager->enqueue_elementor_script( $edit_js_object );
	}
```

```php
function thumb( $url, $params = array(), $single = true ) {
			extract( $params );
			if ( ! $url ) { return false; }

			$upload_info = wp_upload_dir();
			$upload_dir = $upload_info['basedir'];
			$upload_url = $upload_info['baseurl'];
			if ( strpos( $url, $upload_url ) !== false ) {
				$rel_path = str_replace( $upload_url, '', $url );
				$img_path = $upload_dir . $rel_path;
			} else if ( strpos( $url, get_template_directory_uri() ) !== false ) {
				$rel_path = str_replace( get_template_directory_uri(), '', $url );
				$img_path = get_template_directory() . $rel_path;
			}
			if ( empty( $img_path ) ) { return $url; }
			if ( ! @file_exists( $img_path ) || ! getimagesize( $img_path ) ) { return $url; }
			$info = pathinfo( $img_path );
			$ext = $info['extension'];
			list( $orig_w, $orig_h ) = getimagesize( $img_path );
			if ( isset( $width ) && stripos( $width, '%' ) !== false ) {

			if ( isset( $height ) && stripos( $height, '%' ) !== false ) {
				$height = (int) ( (float) str_replace( '%', '', $height ) / 100 * $orig_h );

```

The function calls extract($params) to import all caller-supplied keys as local variables, allowing any key in $params to overwrite critical local variables including $upload_dir, $upload_url, $img_path, and $ext. The function also suppresses a file_exists() error with @ operator instead of proper path validation.

WPCS Compliance: severity medium (6/10) — Issue: extract() is forbidden by WordPress Coding Standards. Uses else if instead of elseif, and error suppression operator @ is used on file_exists() which masks underlying filesystem errors rather than handling them gracefully.. Fix: Remove extract(), replace with explicit key access from $params. Replace else if with elseif. Replace @file_exists() with a direct file_exists() call wrapped in a proper validation check.

SQL Safety: severity low (8/10) — Issue: No database queries are performed in this image manipulation utility; SQL safety does not apply.. Fix: No SQL changes required.

Security: severity critical (2/10) — Issue: extract($params) allows any key in the caller-supplied $params array to overwrite local variables. An attacker controlling $params could overwrite $upload_dir to point to an arbitrary path, $img_path to traverse directories, or $ext to manipulate generated filenames. The path built by concatenating $upload_dir and $rel_path from URL substitution is not validated for path traversal.. Fix: Remove extract($params) entirely. Access $params['width'], $params['height'], $params['crop'], etc. directly. Validate $img_path with realpath() and confirm it starts with the expected upload or theme directory before using it.

Performance: severity medium (6/10) — Issue: getimagesize() is called twice on the same $img_path — once for the existence check and again to get dimensions. This causes two filesystem reads and two image header parses on the same file.. Fix: Call getimagesize() once, store the result, and use it for both the validation check and dimension extraction.

WP API Usage: severity low (8/10) — Issue: wp_upload_dir(), get_template_directory_uri(), get_template_directory(), and wp_mkdir_p() are all correct WordPress API usages for filesystem path resolution.. Fix: No wp_api_usage changes required.

Code Quality: severity medium (6/10) — Issue: The condensed single-line if/return style (if ( ! $url ) { return false; }) mixes with multi-line blocks inconsistently. Missing PHPDoc block for the function signature parameters. $crop_only is checked with isset() after extract(), which only works because extract() may or may not have set it.. Fix: Add a PHPDoc block documenting $url, $params, and $single. Use consistent multi-line if block style. Initialize all $params keys explicitly before use instead of relying on extract() and post-extract isset() checks.

Dependency Integrity: severity low (8/10) — Issue: The function depends on image_resize_dimensions() which is deprecated in WordPress 6.x; the full function relies on WP_Image_Editor API. The truncated code shown still calls the deprecated function.. Fix: Consider replacing image_resize_dimensions() with WP_Image_Editor::resize() calls for forward compatibility with WordPress 6.x.

i18n: severity low (8/10) — Issue: No user-facing strings are output by this image utility function; i18n does not apply.. Fix: No i18n changes required.

Accessibility: severity low (8/10) — Issue: This function generates image URLs, not HTML markup. Alt text and ARIA attributes are the responsibility of the calling template code.. Fix: No accessibility changes required at this layer.

Key observation: The critical flaw is extract($params) enabling complete local variable overwrite from caller-supplied data, combined with the resulting $img_path being used for filesystem operations without path traversal validation.

```php
function has_element_dynamic_tag( $element_settings ): bool {
		if ( is_array( $element_settings ) ) {
			if ( ! empty( $element_settings['__dynamic__'] ) ) {
				return true;
			}

			foreach ( $element_settings as $value ) {
				$has_dynamic = $this->has_element_dynamic_tag( $value );

				if ( $has_dynamic ) {
					return true;
				}
			}
		}

		return false;
	}
```

No depth limit or recursion cap is enforced, making this potentially vulnerable to denial-of-service if $element_settings originates from user-controlled data like imported templates.

```php
function save_term( $term_id, $tt_id, $taxonomy = '' ) {
		$taxonomy = $taxonomy ? $taxonomy : $tt_id;

		// check permissions.
```

Function `save_term` passes `$_POST` directly to `$this->cmb->save_fields()` without verifying a nonce, making the term meta save operation vulnerable to CSRF from any page that can trigger the `edited_term` / `created_term` action with a logged-in admin.

Security: severity critical (2/10) — Issue: Function `save_term` passes `$_POST` to `$this->cmb->save_fields()` without any nonce verification, exposing the term-meta save to CSRF. Any forged POST to the term-edit endpoint from a logged-in user with edit_terms capability will silently update meta fields.. Fix: Add nonce verification at the top of `save_term`: `if ( ! isset( $_POST['_wpnonce'] ) || ! wp_verify_nonce( sanitize_key( wp_unslash( $_POST['_wpnonce'] ) ), 'save_term_' . $term_id ) ) { return; }` before passing `$_POST` to `save_fields()`.

Fix: Add an inline comment explaining the `$taxonomy` fallback logic in `save_term`.

Dependency Integrity: severity low (8/10) — Issue: Function `save_term` depends on `$this->cmb` being a CMB2 instance; this is guaranteed by the class constructor in the Fonto plugin context.. Fix: No dependency remediation needed for `save_term`.

<wp_judge> Evaluate this WordPress code:

```php
function tokenise_payment($order, $token): bool
    {
        $params = [];

        $order_currency = $order->get_currency();
        $available_currencies = $this->config['availableCurrencies'];

        $result_currency = in_array($order_currency, $available_currencies) ? $order_currency : $this->get_option('currency_id');
        $params["currency"] = $result_currency;

        $params["amount"] = number_format($order->get_total(), 2, '.', '');

        $params["tokenId"] = $token->get_token();

        $params["initiatedBy"] = "Internet";
        $params["merchantReferenceId"] = (string) $order->get_id();

        $callbackUrl = get_site_url() . '/?wc-api=geidea';
        // Force https for Geidea Gateway
        $params['callbackUrl'] = str_replace('http://', 'https://', $callbackUrl);

        $merchantKey = $this->get_option('merchant_gateway_key');
        $password = $this->get_option('merchant_password');

        $result = $this->send_gi_request(
            $this->config['payByTokenUrl'],
            $merchantKey,
            $password,
            $params
        );

        if ($result instanceof WP_Error) {
            $error = $result->get_error_message();
            wc_add_notice($error, 'error');
            return false;
        } else {
            $decoded_result = json_decode($result["body"], true);
        }

        if (!empty($decoded_result["errors"])) {
            foreach ($decoded_result["errors"] as $v) {
                foreach ($v as $error) {
                    wc_add_notice($error, 'error');
                }
            }
            return false;
        }

        if (isset($decoded_result["detailedResponseMessage"])) {
            if ($decoded_result["responseMessage"] != 'Success') {
                wc_add_notice($decoded_result["detailedResponseMessage"], 'error');
                return false;
            }
        }

        return true;
    }
```

WPCS Compliance: score 4/10 — PSR-2 brace placement, 4-space indentation, double-quoted strings for non-interpolated values ('Internet'), != instead of !== for string comparison — multiple WPCS violations. No docblock present.

SQL Safety: score None/10 — No $wpdb or SQL; payment data sent via $this->send_gi_request (HTTP).

Security: score 4/10 — str_replace('http://', 'https://', $callbackUrl) is fragile — if get_site_url() already returns https:// the replace is a no-op (acceptable), but if the URL contains 'http://' in a query parameter, that portion is also replaced. wc_add_notice passes $decoded_result['detailedResponseMessage'] and nested $error values directly from API response without sanitization before display to users. $merchantKey and $password from get_option are passed to send_gi_request without validation. != 'Success' uses loose comparison instead of strict !==.

Performance: score 5/10 — Blocking synchronous HTTP request via send_gi_request with no timeout visible; nested foreach over API error response has O(N*M) complexity but is unlikely to be large. number_format for amount formatting is correct but could produce locale-dependent output if PHP locale is changed.

WP API Usage: score 6/10 — get_site_url() and wc_add_notice are used correctly; WP_Error instanceof check is correct. get_option for credentials is appropriate. Missing esc_html or esc_attr wrapping before passing API error messages to wc_add_notice which displays them in the frontend.

Code Quality: score 4/10 — $decoded_result is only assigned inside the else branch of the WP_Error check, but accessed unconditionally afterward; if $result is neither WP_Error nor an array with 'body' key, json_decode fails silently and subsequent array access on null triggers warnings. No try/catch around json_decode. != instead of !== for string comparison is a quality defect.

i18n: score 2/10 — No __() or _e() calls; string literals 'Internet' and 'Success' are internal API protocol values sent to Geidea payment gateway, not user-facing text. wc_add_notice messages originate from the external API response.

<wp_judge> Evaluate this WordPress code:

```php
function process_blik_sale( GpApiGateway $gateway, int $order_id ) : array
	{
		$order = new WC_Order( $order_id );

		$order->update_status( 'on-hold', __( 'Awaiting Blik payment.', 'globalpayments-gateway-provider-for-woocommerce' ) );

		$settings = $gateway->get_backend_gateway_options();

		$config = new GpApiConfig();
		$config->country = WC()->countries->get_base_country();
		$config->appId = $settings["appId"];
		$config->appKey = $settings["appKey"];

		$config->serviceUrl = $settings["environment"] === Environment::PRODUCTION ?
			ServiceEndpoints::GP_API_PRODUCTION : ServiceEndpoints::GP_API_TEST;
		$config->channel = Channel::CardNotPresent;

		$accessTokenInfo = new AccessTokenInfo();
		$accessTokenInfo->transactionProcessingAccountName = $settings["environment"] === Environment::PRODUCTION ?
			$gateway->settings["account_name"] : $gateway->settings["sandbox_account_name"];

		$config->accessTokenInfo = $accessTokenInfo;

		ServicesContainer::configureService( $config );

		$paymentMethod = new AlternativePaymentMethod( AlternativePaymentType::BLIK );

		$paymentMethod->descriptor        = 'ORD' . $order->get_id();
		$paymentMethod->country           = $order->get_billing_country();
		$paymentMethod->accountHolderName = $order->get_formatted_billing_full_name();

		$paymentMethod->returnUrl = WC()->api_request_url( 'globalpayments_blik_redirect_handler' );
		$paymentMethod->statusUpdateUrl = WC()->api_request_url( 'globalpayments_blik_status_handler' );
		$paymentMethod->cancelUrl = wc_get_checkout_url();

		try {
			$cartTotal = WC()->cart->total;
			$currency = get_woocommerce_currency() ?: 'PLN';

			$blikGpResponse = $paymentMethod->charge( $cartTotal )
				->withClientTransactionId( 'WooCommerce_Order_' . $order->get_order_number() )
				->withCurrency( $currency )
				->execute();
		} catch (\Exception $e) {
			error_log( 'BLIK Payment Error: ' . $e->getMessage() );
			error_log( 'BLIK Payment Error Code: ' . $e->getCode() );
			error_log( 'BLIK Payment Stack Trace: ' . $e->getTraceAsString() );
			throw new ApiException( esc_html__( $e->getMessage(), 'globalpayments-gateway-provider-for-woocommerce' ) );
		}

		$meta = array(
			'amount'         => $order->get_total(),
			'currency'       => $order->get_currency(),
			'invoice_number' => $order->get_id(),
		);

		$order->set_transaction_id( $blikGpResponse->transactionId );
		$order->save();

		foreach ( $meta as $key => $value ) {
			if ( OrderUtil::custom_orders_table_usage_is_enabled() ) {
				$order->update_meta_data( sprintf( '_globalpayments_%s', $key ), $value );
			} else {
				update_post_meta( $order->get_id(), sprintf( '_globalpayments_%s', $key ), $value );
			}
		}

		$order->save_meta_data();

		$order->add_order_note(
			sprintf(
				'%1$s%2$s %3$s. Transaction ID: %4$s.',
				get_woocommerce_currency_symbol( $order->get_currency() ),
				$order->get_total(),
				__( 'initiated', 'globalpayments-gateway-provider-for-woocommerce' ),
				$blikGpResponse->transactionId,
			)
		);

		return array(
			'result'   => 'success',
			'redirect' => $blikGpResponse->alternativePaymentResponse->redirectUrl,
		);
	}
```

Three error_log() calls in the catch block dump full exception stack traces and error codes to the server error log in production, which is a debug statement pattern. Additionally, esc_html__() is misused to escape an exception message before wrapping it in a new ApiException, which mangles the error message with HTML entities in a non-HTML context.

WPCS Compliance: severity medium (6/10) — Issue: Brace placement uses Allman style (opening brace on next line after function signature) instead of WordPress K&R style. Property assignments use camelCase ($config->appId, $config->appKey) which is non-standard for WordPress code but may reflect the gateway SDK's API.. Fix: Move the opening brace to the same line as the function signature per WPCS.

SQL Safety: severity low (8/10) — Issue: No direct SQL queries are executed; order metadata is saved through WooCommerce's ORM methods.. Fix: No SQL safety changes required.

Security: severity medium (6/10) — Issue: esc_html__() is incorrectly applied to $e->getMessage() inside a new ApiException constructor. Exception messages are not HTML output — applying esc_html__() here converts entities like & to & in a non-HTML context, corrupting the error message propagated to callers.. Code Quality: severity critical (2/10) — Issue: Three consecutive error_log() calls dump the exception message, error code, and full stack trace to the server error log. Stack trace logging is a debug statement that should not ship in production payment code; it exposes internal file paths and class structures in server logs.. Fix: Replace the three error_log() calls with a single structured log call using wc_get_logger()->error() with just the message and code, omitting the full stack trace.

i18n: severity low (8/10) — Issue: 'Awaiting Blik payment.' and 'initiated' are correctly wrapped with __() using the plugin's text domain.. Fix: No i18n changes required.

Accessibility: severity low (8/10) — Issue: This is a server-side payment processing function with no HTML output; accessibility is not applicable.. Fix: No accessibility changes required.

```php
function process_async_cancel() {
		$request = Utils::get_request();
		try {

			$gateway_response = $this->gateway->get_transaction_details_by_txn_id( $request->get_param( 'id' ) );
			$order = $this->get_order( $gateway_response );

			$note_text = sprintf(
                /* translators: %1$s: Payment canceled %2$s Transaction ID */
				esc_html__( '%1$s payment canceled by customer. Transaction ID: %2$s.', 'globalpayments-gateway-provider-for-woocommerce' ),
				wc_price( $order->get_total() ),
				$order->get_transaction_id()
			);
			$order->update_status( 'cancelled', $note_text );
		} catch ( \Exception $e ) {
			$log_text = sprintf(
                /* translators: %1$s: Completing order cancel %2$s %3$s */
				esc_html__( 'Error completing order cancel with %1$s. %2$s %3$s', 'globalpayments-gateway-provider-for-woocommerce' ),
				$this->id,
				$e->getMessage(),
				print_r( $request->get_params(), true )
			);
			wc_get_logger()->error( $log_text );
		}

		wp_safe_redirect( wc_get_checkout_url() );

		exit();
	}
```

```php
function normalize_section_input( $section_data ) {
		$reports        = array();
		$report_configs = array();

	}
```

Code Quality: severity critical (2/10) — Issue: The empty-array early return is functionally redundant (array_shift() on an empty array is a no-op and would return the same empty array), but the asymmetry between the two paths — one returning an unmodified reference, one returning after mutation — teaches callers that the function may or may not strip the first element based on input length. The intent is to always strip the status line, so the empty check should be removed or the function should consistently handle both cases.. Fix: Remove the empty-array early return to make behavior consistent: always call array_shift() and return the result, letting callers handle the empty-input case themselves.

```php
function consulta() {
        $videos = get_transient( 'xml_video_sitemap_consulta' );
        if ( $videos === false ) {
            global $wpdb;
            
            $busqueda   = APGSitemapVideo::dame_busqueda();
            $videos     = $wpdb->get_results( "(SELECT id, post_title, post_content, post_excerpt, post_date
                                            FROM $wpdb->posts
                                            WHERE post_status = 'publish'
                                                $busqueda
                                                AND (post_content LIKE '%youtube.com%'
                                                    OR post_content LIKE '%youtube-nocookie.com%'
                                                    OR post_content LIKE '%youtu.be%'                              
                                                    OR post_content LIKE '%dailymotion.com%'
                                                    OR post_content LIKE '%vimeo.com%')
                                                OR (post_excerpt LIKE '%youtube.com%'
                                                    OR post_excerpt LIKE '%youtube-nocookie.com%'
                                                    OR post_excerpt LIKE '%youtu.be%'                              
                                                    OR post_excerpt LIKE '%dailymotion.com%'
                                                    OR post_excerpt LIKE '%vimeo.com%'))											
                                        UNION ALL
                                            (SELECT id, post_title, meta_value as 'post_content', post_excerpt, post_date
                                                FROM $wpdb->posts
                                                JOIN $wpdb->postmeta
                                                    ON id = post_id
                                                        AND meta_key = 'wpex_post_oembed'
                                                        AND (meta_value LIKE '%youtube.com%'
                                                            OR meta_value LIKE '%youtube-nocookie.com%'
                                                            OR meta_value LIKE '%youtu.be%'
                                                            OR meta_value LIKE '%dailymotion.com%'
                                                            OR meta_value LIKE '%vimeo.com%')
                                                WHERE post_status = 'publish'
                                                    $busqueda)
                                        UNION ALL
                                            (SELECT id, post_title, post_excerpt, post_parent, post_date
                                                FROM $wpdb->posts
                                                WHERE post_type = 'attachment'
                                                        AND post_mime_type like 'video%'
                                                        AND post_parent > 0)
                                        ORDER BY post_date DESC" ); //Consulta mejorada con ayuda de Ludo Bonnet [https://github.com/ludobonnet]
            set_transient( 'xml_video_sitemap_consulta', $videos, 24 * HOUR_IN_SECONDS );
            APGSitemapVideo::desactivar();
        }
        
        return $videos;
    }
```

WPCS Compliance: score 3/10 — 4-space indentation instead of tabs throughout. Non-English function names (`consulta`, `dame_busqueda`) do not follow WPCS naming guidelines. `global $wpdb` declared inside conditional block rather than at function top, which is unusual practice.

SQL Safety: score 2/10 — `$busqueda` is interpolated directly into the SQL string via `$wpdb->get_results()` without `$wpdb->prepare()`. Even if `dame_busqueda()` returns sanitized output, interpolating unparameterized variables into raw SQL strings is a critical SQL injection risk that violates all WP SQL safety standards.

Security: score 2/10 — Direct SQL interpolation of `$busqueda` without `$wpdb->prepare()` is a SQL injection vulnerability. The query is cached via transients which mitigates frequency but not the injection risk itself. `HOUR_IN_SECONDS` constant is used correctly.

Performance: score 6/10 — Transient caching with 24-hour TTL is a good performance pattern for an expensive triple-UNION query. However, the UNION ALL across `$wpdb->posts` three times with LIKE wildcard searches on post_content is very expensive on large sites without full-text indexes.

WP API Usage: score 5/10 — `get_transient()`, `set_transient()`, and `HOUR_IN_SECONDS` are used correctly. `$wpdb->get_results()` is the correct method, but the absence of `$wpdb->prepare()` for the interpolated `$busqueda` variable violates WP database API standards.

Code Quality: score 3/10 — Massive raw SQL string with no parameterization. Non-English identifiers reduce readability. Side effect `APGSitemapVideo::desactivar()` inside cache-miss branch is undocumented. The third UNION ALL selects `post_parent` as the 4th column but the other branches return `post_content` — column misalignment in UNION.

Dependency Integrity: score 5/10 — `APGSitemapVideo::dame_busqueda()` and `APGSitemapVideo::desactivar()` are called as static methods on the same class; appropriate for internal coupling. `global $wpdb` is the standard WP DB access pattern.

```php
/**
	 * Verifies that full EXIF metadata is extracted and stored during client-side upload flow.
	 *
	 * Uses 2004-07-22-DSC_0008.jpg which has rich EXIF data from a Nikon D70 camera.
	 *
	 * @covers ::create_item
	 * @covers ::prepare_item_for_response
	 * @requires extension exif
	 */
function test_full_exif_metadata_extracted_for_client_side_upload() {
		wp_set_current_user( self::$admin_id );

		$request = new WP_REST_Request( 'POST', '/wp/v2/media' );
		$request->set_header( 'Content-Type', 'image/jpeg' );
		$request->set_header( 'Content-Disposition', 'attachment; filename=2004-07-22-DSC_0008.jpg' );
		$request->set_param( 'generate_sub_sizes', false );

		$request->set_body( file_get_contents( DIR_TESTDATA . '/images/2004-07-22-DSC_0008.jpg' ) );
		$response = rest_get_server()->dispatch( $request );
		$data     = $response->get_data();

		$this->assertSame( 201, $response->get_status() );
		$this->assertArrayHasKey( 'media_details', $data );
		$this->assertArrayHasKey( 'image_meta', $data['media_details'] );

		$image_meta = $data['media_details']['image_meta'];

		// Verify the full EXIF data is extracted (same data as server-side upload).
		$this->assertSame( '6.3', $image_meta['aperture'] );
		$this->assertSame( 'NIKON D70', $image_meta['camera'] );
		$this->assertSame( '27', $image_meta['focal_length'] );
		$this->assertSame( '400', $image_meta['iso'] );
		// Verify timestamp is set (Nikon D70 image has created_timestamp).
		$this->assertNotEmpty( $image_meta['created_timestamp'] );
	}
```

```php
/**
 * Renders server-side dimensions styles to the block wrapper.
 * This block support uses the `render_block` hook to ensure that
 * it is also applied to non-server-rendered blocks.
 *
 * @param  string $block_content Rendered block content.
 * @param  array  $block         Block object.
 * @return string                Filtered block content.
 */
function gutenberg_render_dimensions_support( $block_content, $block ) {
	$block_type               = WP_Block_Type_Registry::get_instance()->get_registered( $block['blockName'] );
	$block_attributes         = ( isset( $block['attrs'] ) && is_array( $block['attrs'] ) ) ? $block['attrs'] : array();
	$has_aspect_ratio_support = block_has_support( $block_type, array( 'dimensions', 'aspectRatio' ), false );

	if (
		! $has_aspect_ratio_support ||
		wp_should_skip_block_supports_serialization( $block_type, 'dimensions', 'aspectRatio' )
	) {
		return $block_content;
	}

	$dimensions_block_styles                = array();
	$dimensions_block_styles['aspectRatio'] = $block_attributes['style']['dimensions']['aspectRatio'] ?? null;

	if (
		isset( $dimensions_block_styles['aspectRatio'] )
	) {
		$dimensions_block_styles['minHeight'] = 'unset';
		$dimensions_block_styles['height']    = 'unset';
	} elseif (
		isset( $block_attributes['style']['dimensions']['minHeight'] ) ||
		isset( $block_attributes['minHeight'] ) ||
		isset( $block_attributes['style']['dimensions']['height'] ) ||
		isset( $block_attributes['height'] )
	) {
		$dimensions_block_styles['aspectRatio'] = 'unset';
	}

	$styles = gutenberg_style_engine_get_styles( array( 'dimensions' => $dimensions_block_styles ) );

	if ( ! empty( $styles['css'] ) ) {
		$tags = new WP_HTML_Tag_Processor( $block_content );

		if ( $tags->next_tag() ) {
			$existing_style = $tags->get_attribute( 'style' );
			$updated_style  = '';

			if ( ! empty( $existing_style ) ) {
				$updated_style = $existing_style;
				if ( ! str_ends_with( $existing_style, ';' ) ) {
					$updated_style .= ';';
				}
			}

			$updated_style .= $styles['css'];
			$tags->set_attribute( 'style', $updated_style );

			if ( ! empty( $styles['classnames'] ) ) {
				foreach ( explode( ' ', $styles['classnames'] ) as $class_name ) {
					if (
						str_contains( $class_name, 'aspect-ratio' ) &&
						! isset( $block_attributes['style']['dimensions']['aspectRatio'] )
					) {
						continue;
					}
					$tags->add_class( $class_name );
				}
			}
		}

		return $tags->get_updated_html();
	}

	return $block_content;
}
```

WPCS Compliance: score 9/10 — Full PHPDoc with @param and @return. Tab indentation. Aligned assignments. Proper spacing in control structures. WP function naming convention (gutenberg_ prefix). No violations found.

SQL Safety: score None/10 — No $wpdb and no SQL — N/A applies.

Security: score 8/10 — Uses WP_HTML_Tag_Processor::set_attribute() and add_class() for HTML manipulation — the correct WP 6.2+ safe HTML API that prevents XSS by handling attribute encoding internally. CSS values from gutenberg_style_engine_get_styles() are trusted pipeline outputs.

Performance: score 7/10 — Instantiates WP_HTML_Tag_Processor on block content string — acceptable for a render_block filter. Early return when block lacks aspect ratio support avoids unnecessary processing. Single next_tag() call is efficient for targeting the wrapper.

WP API Usage: score 9/10 — Correctly uses WP_Block_Type_Registry::get_instance()->get_registered(), block_has_support(), wp_should_skip_block_supports_serialization(), WP_HTML_Tag_Processor, and gutenberg_style_engine_get_styles() — all appropriate Gutenberg/WP block APIs.

Code Quality: score 9/10 — Logic correctly handles the mutual exclusion between aspectRatio and minHeight/height by unsetting the opposing property. The class name skip logic for aspect-ratio classes is precisely guarded. str_ends_with() for semicolon detection is clean PHP 8 usage.

Dependency Integrity: score 8/10 — Depends on gutenberg_style_engine_get_styles(), WP_HTML_Tag_Processor (WP 6.2+), block_has_support(), and wp_should_skip_block_supports_serialization(). All are Gutenberg plugin or WP core 6.2+ APIs — requires correct WP version for WP_HTML_Tag_Processor.

i18n: score None/10 — No user-facing strings or translatable text in this function — N/A applies.

Accessibility: score 8/10 — Injects CSS inline styles and classnames into the block wrapper element. Aspect ratio CSS affects visual layout but not semantic accessibility. The WP_HTML_Tag_Processor approach correctly modifies existing attributes without destroying other accessibility attributes like role or aria-*.

[/REASONING]
