<wp_judge> Evaluate this WordPress code:

```php
function dispatch() {
		preg_match( '#\/wp-json/(.*)\??.*#i', $_SERVER['REQUEST_URI'], $route_match );
		if ( ! isset( $route_match[1] ) ) {
			return;
		}
		$f          = explode( '?', $route_match[1] );
		$rest_route = '/' . $f[0];

		// Authentication isn’t performed when making internal requests.
		$request = new \WP_REST_Request( $_SERVER['REQUEST_METHOD'], $rest_route );
		$request->set_query_params( $_GET );

		if ( 'POST' === $_SERVER['REQUEST_METHOD'] ) {
			$body = json_decode( file_get_contents( 'php://input' ), true );
			$request->set_body_params( $body );

			if ( ! empty( $_FILES['file'] ) ) {
				self::upload_media();
			}
		}

		$response      = rest_do_request( $request );
		$server        = rest_get_server();
		$response_data = $server->response_to_data( $response, false );

		// Save custom meta.
		if ( 'POST' === $_SERVER['REQUEST_METHOD'] ) {
			if ( ! empty( $body['meta'] ) ) {
				$content_type = self::get_content_type( $rest_route );
				self::update_meta( $response_data['id'], $content_type, $body['meta'] );
			}
		}
		self::send_response( $response, $response_data );
	}
```

The dispatch method constructs an internal REST request bypassing WordPress authentication and nonce verification entirely, and the comment explicitly notes authentication is skipped — this is the critical defect enabling unauthenticated state changes.

WPCS Compliance: severity low (8/10) — Issue: Missing PHPDoc block on dispatch(); $_SERVER superglobal is accessed without isset() checks on REQUEST_METHOD and REQUEST_URI.. Fix: Add PHPDoc block and wrap $_SERVER accesses with isset() checks.

SQL Safety: severity low (8/10) — Issue: No raw SQL in dispatch(); meta updates delegate to self::update_meta() which should use wpdb->prepare() internally.. Fix: No SQL changes needed in dispatch(); ensure update_meta() uses prepared statements.

Security: severity critical (2/10) — Issue: dispatch() explicitly bypasses WordPress REST authentication ('Authentication isn't performed when making internal requests') and processes POST requests including file uploads and meta updates with no nonce, no capability check, and no authentication; any caller can trigger privileged REST operations.. Fix: Add a shared-secret or nonce verification at the top of dispatch() using wp_verify_nonce() with a server-generated token, or restrict dispatch() to authenticated internal callers only via current_user_can().

Performance: severity low (8/10) — Issue: file_get_contents('php://input') is called unconditionally for all POST requests; efficient for single-pass body reading.. Fix: No performance changes needed.

WP API Usage: severity medium (6/10) — Issue: Constructs WP_REST_Request without setting authentication context; WordPress REST server requires proper authentication to enforce route permission_callbacks. Bypassing this means permission_callbacks on the matched routes are also skipped.. Fix: Set authentication headers or use the REST server's authenticate() method before calling rest_do_request() to ensure permission_callbacks are enforced.

Code Quality: severity medium (6/10) — Issue: $body is set inside the POST branch but referenced outside it in the meta-saving block; if REQUEST_METHOD is not POST, $body is undefined and the second POST check accesses an undefined variable.. Fix: Initialize $body = null; before the conditional block to prevent undefined variable access.

Dependency Integrity: severity low (8/10) — Issue: Depends on self::upload_media(), self::get_content_type(), self::update_meta(), self::send_response() as static methods; all appear to be plugin-internal methods.. Fix: No dependency changes needed.

i18n: severity low (8/10) — Issue: No translatable strings; dispatch() produces no user-visible output directly.. Fix: No i18n changes needed.

Accessibility: severity low (8/10) — Issue: No HTML output; server-side request dispatcher.. Fix: No accessibility changes needed.

```php
/**
		 * Fires just before the target post is updated with its new data.
		 * Allows for final data adjustments before updating the target post.
		 *
		 * @module copy-post
		 *
		 * @since 7.0.0
		 *
		 * @param array $data Post data with which to update the target (new) post.
		 * @param WP_Post $source_post Post object being copied.
		 * @param int     $target_post_id Target post ID.
		 */
function update_content( $source_post, $target_post_id ) {
		$data = array(
			'ID'             => $target_post_id,
			'post_title'     => $source_post->post_title,
			'post_content'   => $source_post->post_content,
			'post_excerpt'   => $source_post->post_excerpt,
			'comment_status' => $source_post->comment_status,
			'ping_status'    => $source_post->ping_status,
			'post_category'  => wp_get_post_categories( $source_post->ID ),
			'post_password'  => $source_post->post_password,
			'tags_input'     => $source_post->tags_input,
		);

		// Copy footnotes with regenerated IDs.
		$data = $this->copy_footnotes( $data, $source_post, $target_post_id );

		
		$data = apply_filters( 'jetpack_copy_post_data', $data, $source_post, $target_post_id );
		return wp_update_post( $data );
	}
```

WPCS Compliance: score 8/10 — Well-aligned WPCS: snake_case naming, proper array alignment with fat-comma alignment, correct use of wp_get_post_categories and wp_update_post. Minor: there is a trailing blank line before apply_filters that suggests a removed code block without cleanup.

Security: score 7/10 — Data flows from a server-side WP_Post object (not user input), through copy_footnotes and the jetpack_copy_post_data filter, then into wp_update_post which handles sanitization internally. The filter hook is a legitimate extension point. post_password is copied verbatim which is acceptable since passwords are stored hashed.

Performance: score 7/10 — wp_get_post_categories issues a database query during array construction. wp_update_post triggers multiple DB writes and fires several action hooks including save_post. For a copy operation this cost is expected and unavoidable, but callers should be aware of the write overhead.

WP API Usage: score 9/10 — Excellent use of WP API surface: wp_get_post_categories for taxonomy retrieval, apply_filters with a namespaced hook jetpack_copy_post_data for extensibility, and wp_update_post for the actual write. All the correct WP APIs for a post-copy operation.

Code Quality: score 8/10 — Clear intent, well-structured data array with aligned keys, proper delegation to copy_footnotes for footnote logic, and extensible via apply_filters. The stray blank line before apply_filters is a minor cleanliness issue.

Dependency Integrity: score 8/10 — Dependencies are WordPress core functions (wp_get_post_categories, wp_update_post, apply_filters) and the internal copy_footnotes method. No third-party libraries introduced; all dependencies are stable WP core APIs.

/**

	 *
	 * @param array $new_instance Values just sent to be saved.

function update( $new_instance, $old_instance ) { // phpcs:ignore VariableAnalysis.CodeAnalysis.VariableAnalysis.UnusedVariable

Performance: severity medium (6/10) — Issue: Function `search_by_title_only` builds SQL inside a `foreach` loop over `$q['search_terms']`, meaning one LIKE term is appended per iteration. /**
		 * Get the question object.
		 *
		 * @since  3.0.0
		 *
		 * @param  mixed $the_question
		 *
		 * @uses   WP_Post
		 * @return WP_Post|bool false on failure
		 */
function get_question_object( $the_question ) {
			if ( false === $the_question ) {
				$the_question = get_post_type() === LP_QUESTION_CPT ? $GLOBALS['post'] : false;
			} elseif ( is_numeric( $the_question ) ) {
				$the_question = get_post( $the_question );
			} elseif ( $the_question instanceof LP_Course_Item ) {
				$the_question = get_post( $the_question->get_id() );
			} elseif ( ! ( $the_question instanceof WP_Post ) ) {
				$the_question = false;
			}

			return apply_filters( 'learn-press/question/post-object', $the_question );
		}
```

The use of $GLOBALS['post'] when $the_question is false reads the global post context — this is a common WP pattern but relies on the global being properly set by the loop. No unsanitized user input is passed to dangerous functions.

Performance: score 7/10 — Calls get_post() when passed a numeric ID or LP_Course_Item, each triggering a DB query if the post is not cached. The false branch reads from $GLOBALS avoiding a DB call. apply_filters fires registered callbacks. Overall acceptable for a single-object retrieval.

WP API Usage: score 8/10 — Uses get_post_type() and get_post() from WP core correctly, checks LP_QUESTION_CPT constant for post type comparison, uses LP_Course_Item and WP_Post instanceof checks appropriately, and wraps the return in apply_filters with a namespaced hook learn-press/question/post-object for extensibility.

Code Quality: score 7/10 — Handles four input types: false, numeric, LP_Course_Item, and WP_Post with explicit fallback to false. The multi-branch resolution is clear and systematic. The apply_filters wrapper enables downstream override. Minor: reliance on $GLOBALS['post'] is implicit and could surprise callers.

Dependency Integrity: score 7/10 — Depends on WP core (get_post_type, get_post, apply_filters), LearnPress constants (LP_QUESTION_CPT) and classes (LP_Course_Item). All are internal to the LearnPress ecosystem with no external library risk.

i18n: score None/10 — No user-facing string literals. All strings are hook names (learn-press/question/post-object) and constant identifiers — not translatable text.

This debug utility outputs raw variable dumps directly to the browser with hardcoded English label strings that are not passed through any translation function, and it has no production guard. WPCS Compliance: severity medium (6/10) — Issue: The function shadows PHP's built-in var_dump() with an incompatible signature inside a class, which causes naming confusion and is flagged by WPCS as a prohibited function name override.. Fix: Rename the method to something like debug_var() or dump_variable() to avoid shadowing the PHP built-in and eliminate the WPCS naming violation.

SQL Safety: severity low (8/10) — Issue: No SQL queries are executed in this function; it only formats and outputs variable data.. Fix: No SQL safety changes required.

Security: severity medium (6/10) — Issue: wp_kses() is called with true as the allowed-tags argument, which is not a valid allowed-tags array and effectively strips all tags, making the <pre> wrapper meaningless. The intent appears to be wp_kses_post() or a custom tags whitelist.. Fix: Replace wp_kses( ..., true ) with esc_html( print_r( $variable, true ) ) wrapped in a <pre> tag, since pre-formatted debug output should be HTML-escaped, not kses-filtered.

Performance: severity low (8/10) — Issue: print_r() with return=true allocates a string representation of the full variable; for large objects this can be expensive, but this is inherent to any debug output function.. Fix: No performance changes required beyond ensuring the function is never called in production.

WP API Usage: severity low (8/10) — Issue: esc_html() is correctly used on $file_path and $line; no WordPress API misuse is present beyond the wp_kses misuse noted under security.. Fix: No additional wp_api_usage changes beyond fixing the wp_kses() call.

Code Quality: severity critical (2/10) — Issue: The function contains no production guard. Debug dump methods must be gated on WP_DEBUG or a plugin-specific constant so they never produce output on live sites. Unconditional debug output is a critical code quality failure.. Fix: Wrap the entire function body in if ( defined( 'WP_DEBUG' ) && WP_DEBUG ) { ... } to ensure output is suppressed on production sites.

Dependency Integrity: severity low (8/10) — Issue: No external dependencies; the function only uses PHP built-ins and WordPress escaping helpers.. Fix: No dependency integrity changes required.

i18n: severity high (4/10) — Issue: The hardcoded strings 'FILE:' and 'LINE:' are output directly without __() or esc_html_e() wrappers, making them untranslatable.. Fix: Wrap both label strings: echo esc_html__( 'FILE:', 'learnpress-bbpress' ) and echo esc_html__( 'LINE:', 'learnpress-bbpress' ) to enable translation.

Accessibility: severity low (8/10) — Issue: Debug output rendered to frontend is not accessible by design, but it should only appear in admin or debug contexts where accessibility is less critical.. Fix: No accessibility changes required beyond ensuring this output only appears in debug/admin contexts.

Key observation: Hardcoded 'FILE:' and 'LINE:' labels are never passed through translation functions, and the entire function body runs unconditionally in production because there is no WP_DEBUG guard — two independent violations that compound each other.

Evaluate this WordPress code: ```php /** * Get edit|view link of a page */ function get_page_permalink() { $page_id = (int) $_REQUEST['page_id'] ?? 0; ?>

The function reads $_REQUEST['page_id'] with a precedence bug, outputs URL values without esc_url(), uses _e() without escaping, and lacks any nonce or capability check before rendering admin edit links.

WPCS Compliance: severity medium (6/10) — Issue: The cast (int) $_REQUEST['page_id'] ?? 0 has operator precedence issue: ?? applies to the result of (int) cast on the undefined key, not to the raw key — if the key is missing, a PHP notice fires before the null-coalescing can act. WPCS also requires esc_url() on all href output.. Fix: Use $page_id = isset( $_REQUEST['page_id'] ) ? absint( $_REQUEST['page_id'] ) : 0; to safely handle the missing-key case before casting.

SQL Safety: severity low (8/10) — Issue: No direct SQL queries are executed; get_edit_post_link() and get_permalink() use WordPress's internal query layer.. Fix: No SQL safety changes required.

Security: severity critical (2/10) — Issue: get_edit_post_link() and get_permalink() return values are echoed directly without esc_url(), allowing XSS if either function returns a javascript: URI or malformed URL in edge cases. Additionally, there is no nonce verification or current_user_can() check before rendering admin edit links.. Fix: Wrap both href outputs with esc_url(): echo esc_url( get_edit_post_link( $page_id ) ) and echo esc_url( get_permalink( $page_id ) ). Add nonce and capability checks at the top of the function.

Performance: severity low (8/10) — Issue: No performance issues; both get_edit_post_link() and get_permalink() use WordPress internal caching.. Fix: No performance changes required.

WP API Usage: severity medium (6/10) — Issue: _e() is used without a preceding esc_html() — while _e() outputs a translated string, best practice per WPCS is esc_html_e() to prevent potential XSS if translated strings contain HTML.. Fix: Replace _e( 'Edit Page', 'learnpress' ) with esc_html_e( 'Edit Page', 'learnpress' ) and similarly for 'View Page'.

Code Quality: severity medium (6/10) — Issue: die() is called at the end of a template-output function; this is an AJAX handler pattern but the function lacks wp_die() which provides proper WordPress shutdown handling, and there is no check verifying this is an AJAX context.. Fix: Replace die() with wp_die() to use WordPress's proper shutdown hook, and add a check_ajax_referer() call at the start of the function.

Dependency Integrity: severity low (8/10) — Issue: The function depends only on core WordPress functions which are always available in a WordPress context.. Fix: No dependency integrity changes required.

i18n: severity low (8/10) — Issue: 'Edit Page' and 'View Page' use the correct learnpress text domain and are properly wrapped for translation.. Fix: No i18n changes required beyond upgrading _e() to esc_html_e() as noted under wp_api_usage.

Accessibility: severity medium (6/10) — Issue: Both links open in _blank without a screen-reader hint (e.g., aria-label or a visually hidden span indicating 'opens in new tab'), which can disorient keyboard and screen reader users.. Fix: Add rel="noopener noreferrer" to both _blank links and consider adding an aria-label that includes context about the target, e.g., aria-label="Edit Page (opens in new tab)".

Key observation: Both href attributes are echoed without esc_url(), which is the primary XSS vector; the missing nonce and capability check means any logged-in user can invoke this AJAX handler and probe page IDs.

```php
/**
 * Update user basic information.
 *
 * @param bool $wp_error - Optional. Return WP_Error object in case updating failed.
 *
 * @return bool|mixed|WP_Error
 */
function learn_press_update_user_profile_basic_information( $wp_error = false ) {
	$user_id = get_current_user_id();

	if ( ! $user_id ) {
		return new WP_Error( 2, 'The user is invalid' );
	}

	$update_data = array(
		'ID'           => $user_id,
		'first_name'   => LP_Request::get_param( 'first_name' ),
		'last_name'    => LP_Request::get_param( 'last_name' ),
		'description'  => LP_Request::get_param( 'description', '', 'html' ),
		'display_name' => LP_Request::get_param( 'account_display_name' ),
		'user_email'   => LP_Request::get_param( 'account_email' ),
	);

	$update_data = apply_filters( 'learn-press/update-profile-basic-information-data', $update_data );
	$update_meta = LP_Helper::sanitize_params_submitted( $_POST['_lp_custom_register'] ?? '' );

	$return = LP_Forms_Handler::update_user_data( $update_data, $update_meta );

	// Update for social.
	$socials    = LP_Request::get_param( 'user_profile_social' );
	$extra_data = get_user_meta( $user_id, '_lp_extra_info', true );

	if ( ! empty( $extra_data ) ) {
		$socials = array_merge( $extra_data, $socials );
	}

	update_user_meta( $user_id, '_lp_extra_info', $socials );

	if ( is_wp_error( $return ) ) {
		return $wp_error ? $return : false;
	}

	return $return;
}
```

Function `learn_press_update_user_profile_basic_information` updates user profile data from `$_POST` and `LP_Request` without verifying a nonce, leaving the profile update endpoint open to CSRF attacks; additionally the WP_Error message uses an untranslated hardcoded English string.

WPCS Compliance: severity low (8/10) — Issue: Function `learn_press_update_user_profile_basic_information` PHPDoc uses `@param bool $wp_error -` with a leading dash which is not WPCS format; `@return bool|mixed|WP_Error` should be `@return bool|WP_Error`.. Fix: Remove the dash from `@param` and simplify `@return` to `@return bool|WP_Error` in the PHPDoc of `learn_press_update_user_profile_basic_information`.

SQL Safety: severity low (8/10) — Issue: Function `learn_press_update_user_profile_basic_information` uses `wp_update_user()` (via `LP_Forms_Handler::update_user_data`) and `update_user_meta()` — both WP core functions that handle escaping internally.. Fix: No SQL remediation needed for `learn_press_update_user_profile_basic_information`.

Security: severity critical (2/10) — Issue: Function `learn_press_update_user_profile_basic_information` updates user profile data from `$_POST` and `LP_Request::get_param()` without any nonce verification. A forged POST request from a browser tab can trigger this function while the user is logged in, silently changing display name, email, and social links.. Fix: Add nonce verification at the start of `learn_press_update_user_profile_basic_information`: `if ( ! isset( $_POST['_lpnonce'] ) || ! wp_verify_nonce( sanitize_key( wp_unslash( $_POST['_lpnonce'] ) ), 'lp_update_profile_' . get_current_user_id() ) ) { return false; }`.

<wp_judge> Evaluate this WordPress code:

```php
/**
		 * @param string $selector
		 *
		 * @return integer
		 */
function getCssSelectorPrecedence( $selector ) {
			$selectorKey = md5( $selector );
			if ( !isset( $this->caches[self::CACHE_KEY_SELECTOR][$selectorKey] ) ) {
				$precedence = 0;
				$value      = 100;
				// ids: worth 100, classes: worth 10, elements: worth 1
				$search = array( '\\#', '\\.', '' );

				foreach ( $search as $s ) {
					if ( trim( $selector == '' ) ) {
						break;
					}
					$number   = 0;
					$selector = preg_replace( '/' . $s . '\\w+/', '', $selector, - 1, $number );
					$precedence += ( $value * $number );
					$value /= 10;
				}
				$this->caches[self::CACHE_KEY_SELECTOR][$selectorKey] = $precedence;
			}

			return $this->caches[self::CACHE_KEY_SELECTOR][$selectorKey];
		}
```

WPCS Compliance: score 4/10 — Uses camelCase method name (getCssSelectorPrecedence) instead of WordPress snake_case convention. Missing space before ! in '!isset'. Space before the negative literal '- 1' is non-standard. The function follows a different coding standard (PSR) throughout.

SQL Safety: score None/10 — No $wpdb or SQL queries; dimension is not applicable.

Security: score 4/10 — The third element in $search is an empty string '', which causes preg_replace('/' . '' . '\w+/', ...) to match every word token in the selector on each iteration — this is a logic bug that would corrupt the precedence calculation. Input CSS selectors are not validated, and a malformed selector could trigger preg_replace errors.

Performance: score 6/10 — MD5 caching is used to avoid recomputing precedence for identical selectors, which is appropriate. However, md5() hashing on every call for uncached selectors adds overhead. The empty string in $search causes an extra preg_replace pass that strips all word tokens, undermining the calculation.

WP API Usage: score 3/10 — No WordPress API is used; all operations are pure PHP. This is a vendored Emogrifier class bundled without WP adaptation. Using md5() instead of a WP cache abstraction (wp_cache_get/set) is a missed WP integration opportunity.

Code Quality: score 3/10 — Critical logic bug: trim($selector == '') compares $selector to an empty string (producing a boolean), then passes that boolean to trim() — this condition is always false unless $selector is literally '1' or truthy, making the break guard non-functional. The empty string in $search produces a match-all regex.

Dependency Integrity: score 5/10 — Relies only on built-in PHP functions (md5, preg_replace, trim) and an internal $caches property. No external dependencies, but the internal $caches structure is assumed to be initialized before use.

i18n: score 2/10 — No user-facing strings exist in this CSS selector parser; the vendored Emogrifier class predates WP i18n integration and any debug output it might surface would require __() wrappers.

Accessibility: score None/10 — No HTML output produced; dimension is not applicable.

/**
		 * Delete relate data when delete course
		 *
		 * @since 4.1.4.1
		 * @author tungnx
		 * @version 1.0.0
		 */
function delete_relate_data_when_delete_course() {
			$lp_section_db    = LP_Section_DB::getInstance();
			$lp_user_items_db = LP_User_Items_DB::getInstance();

			try {
				// Check valid user
				if ( ! current_user_can( 'administrator' ) && $this->get_author( 'id' ) !== get_current_user_id() ) {
					return;
				}

				$section_ids = $lp_section_db->get_section_ids_by_course( $this->get_id() );

				$filter                   = new LP_Section_Filter();
				$filter->section_ids      = $section_ids;
				$filter->author_id_course = $this->get_author( 'id' );

				// Delete section
				$lp_section_db->delete_section( $filter );
				// Delete section items
				$lp_section_db->delete_section_items( $filter );

				$filter_user_items          = new LP_User_Items_Filter();
				$filter_user_items->item_id = $this->get_id();
				$user_course_ids            = $lp_user_items_db->get_user_items_by_course( $filter_user_items );

				$this->delete_user_item_and_result( $user_course_ids );

				// Clear cache total students enrolled.
				$lp_course_cache = new LP_Course_Cache( true );
				$lp_course_cache->clean_total_students_enrolled( $this->get_id() );
				$lp_course_cache->clean_total_students_enrolled_or_purchased( $this->get_id() );
				// Clear cache count students many courses.
				$lp_courses_cache = new LP_Courses_Cache( true );
				$lp_courses_cache->clear_cache_on_group( LP_Courses_Cache::KEYS_COUNT_STUDENT_COURSES );
				$lp_course_cache->clear_cache_on_group( LP_Courses_Cache::KEYS_COUNT_COURSES_FREE );
				// Clear cache user course.
				$lp_user_items_cache = new LP_User_Items_Cache();
				$lp_user_items_cache->clean_user_items_by_course( $this->get_id() );
			} catch ( Throwable $e ) {
				error_log( __FUNCTION__ . ':' . $e->getMessage() );
			}
		}
```

WPCS Compliance: score 6/10 — Three-level indentation (tabs) suggests nesting inside a class method correctly; alignment padding on assignment operators follows WPCS. Docblock is present but @param is missing (function takes no params — correct), though class @since version mismatch between docblock style and code version is a minor issue.

/**
	 * HTML for tom select.
	 *
	 * @param array $args {
	 *     Arguments.
	 *
	 *     @type array  $options    Options for select.
	 *     @type string $name       Name attribute for select.
	 *     @type string $class_name Class name for select.
	 * }
	 *
	 * @return string
	 * @since 4.3.0
	 * @version 1.0.0
	 */
function html_tom_select( array $args = [] ): string {
		$html_options = '';

		$options       = $args['options'] ?? [];
		$name          = $args['name'] ?? '';
		$class_name    = $args['class_name'] ?? '';
		$default_value = $args['default_value'] ?? '';
		$multiple      = $args['multiple'] ?? false;
		$multiple      = $multiple ? 'multiple' : '';
		foreach ( $options as $key => $value ) {
			if ( is_array( $default_value ) ) {
				$selected = in_array( $key, $default_value, true ) ? 'selected' : '';
			} else {
				$selected = selected( $default_value, $key, false );
			}

			$html_options .= sprintf( '<option value="%s" %s>%s</option>', esc_attr( $key ), $selected, esc_html( $value ) );
		}

		$section = [
			'select'     => sprintf(
				'<select name="%s" class="%s lp-tom-select" %s>',
				esc_attr( $name ),
				esc_attr( $class_name ),
				$multiple
			),
			'options'    => $html_options,
			'select-end' => '</select>',
		];

		return Template::combine_components( $section );
	}

/**
	 * Create answers for a question.
	 *
	 * @param int    $question_id
	 * @param string $type
	 */
function create_question_answers( $question_id, $type ) {
		global $wpdb;

		$answers = $this->get_answers( $type );
		foreach ( $answers as $order => $answer ) {
			$data = array(
				'question_id' => $question_id,
				'title'       => $answer['title'],
				'value'       => $answer['value'],
				'is_true'     => $answer['is_true'],
				'order'       => $order + 1,
			);

			$wpdb->insert(
				$wpdb->learnpress_question_answers,
				$data,
				array( '%d', '%s', '%s', '%s', '%d' )
			);
		}
	}
```

The function issues one $wpdb->insert() call per answer row inside a foreach loop, creating an N+1 database write pattern. For a question with many answers, this produces unnecessary round-trips that should be batched into a single multi-row INSERT.

WPCS Compliance: severity low (8/10) — Issue: PHPDoc block is missing @return tag. The parameter types in @param annotations should match the actual PHP types used.. Fix: Add @return void to the PHPDoc block.

SQL Safety: severity medium (6/10) — Issue: $wpdb->insert() automatically escapes values via the format array, so individual values are safe. However, batching with a raw multi-row INSERT would require $wpdb->prepare() with explicit placeholders to maintain safety.. Fix: When refactoring to a batch INSERT, use $wpdb->prepare() with a repeated placeholder pattern for each row to maintain SQL safety.

Security: severity low (8/10) — Issue: No user-supplied superglobal data is used directly; $question_id and $type come from function parameters. The function should be called only from contexts that have already validated capability.. Fix: No additional security changes required within this function; capability checks belong in the calling code.

Performance: severity critical (2/10) — Issue: Each iteration of the foreach loop executes a separate database INSERT. For a question with 10 answer choices, this is 10 round-trips where 1 would suffice. This N+1 write pattern degrades performance significantly at scale.. Fix: Collect all row data into an array, then build a single multi-row INSERT statement using $wpdb->prepare() with repeated %d/%s placeholder groups and execute once outside the loop.

WP API Usage: severity low (8/10) — Issue: $wpdb->insert() is the correct WordPress API for single-row inserts; the format array is correctly specified.. Fix: No wp_api_usage changes required for the current pattern; the batch refactor uses $wpdb->prepare() and $wpdb->query() which are also correct WP APIs.

Code Quality: severity medium (6/10) — Issue: No error handling is present; $wpdb->insert() returns false on failure but the return value is discarded, so silent failures on any answer row go undetected.. Fix: Check the return value of the insert (or query) and log or throw on failure: if ( false === $wpdb->insert(...) ) { ... handle error ... }

Dependency Integrity: severity low (8/10) — Issue: The function relies on $wpdb->learnpress_question_answers being a registered custom table. If the table registration is missing, the insert silently fails.. Fix: No dependency integrity changes required within this function; table registration belongs in the plugin activation hook.

i18n: severity low (8/10) — Issue: No user-facing strings are output in this function; it only performs database writes.. Fix: No i18n changes required.

Accessibility: severity low (8/10) — Issue: This is a data-layer function with no HTML output; accessibility is not applicable.. Fix: No accessibility changes required.

Key observation: One database INSERT per loop iteration is the defining defect; replacing the loop body with a batch INSERT using a single $wpdb->prepare() call eliminates the N+1 pattern and is the required fix.

/**
	 * Lock process
	 *
	 * Lock the process so that multiple instances can't run simultaneously.
	 * Override if applicable, but the duration should be greater than that
	 * defined in the time_exceeded() method.
	 */
function lock_process() {
		$this->start_time = time(); // Set start time of current process.

		$lock_duration = ( property_exists( $this, 'queue_lock_time' ) ) ? $this->queue_lock_time : 60; // 1 minute
		$lock_duration = apply_filters( $this->identifier . '_queue_lock_time', $lock_duration );

		set_site_transient( $this->identifier . '_process_lock', microtime(), $lock_duration );
	}
```

WPCS Compliance: score 8/10 — Snake_case method name, correct inline comments, proper use of property_exists and ternary. Uses set_site_transient following WP API conventions. Tab indentation is correct. Minor: ternary condition wraps in unnecessary outer parentheses but this is cosmetic.

SQL Safety: score 3/10 — No direct SQL queries constructed. set_site_transient writes to the database via WP transients API internally, making this function an indirect DB writer. Scored rather than null because a DB write occurs every time lock_process runs.

Security: score 6/10 — The lock key $this->identifier . '_process_lock' relies on $this->identifier being unique and unguessable. If $this->identifier is user-influenced, an attacker could craft a lock key collision to block legitimate background processes. apply_filters on the lock duration allows third-party code to set duration to 0, effectively disabling the lock.

Performance: score 7/10 — set_site_transient writes to the database (or object cache if available). microtime() is used as the lock value — appropriate for timestamping. The property_exists check avoids a PHP notice if queue_lock_time is not set. One DB write per lock acquisition is expected and unavoidable.

WP API Usage: score 9/10 — Correctly uses set_site_transient for multisite-aware process locking, apply_filters with a dynamic namespaced hook for configurable lock duration, and property_exists for defensive property check. All are idiomatic WP API usage for background processing.

Code Quality: score 8/10 — Clean implementation of the WP Background Processing lock pattern. The apply_filters call allows plugins to configure lock duration per-process. The microtime() stored as value enables potential debugging of lock acquisition time. Default 60-second fallback is documented in the comment.

Dependency Integrity: score 8/10 — Depends on WP core functions set_site_transient, apply_filters, property_exists, time, and microtime. All are WP core or PHP built-in — no external library dependencies.

i18n: score None/10 — No user-facing strings present. All string literals are dynamic lock key suffixes (_queue_lock_time, _process_lock) used as internal identifiers.

/**
		 * Duplicate course, lesson, quiz, question.
		 *
		 * @since 3.0.0
		 *
		 * @note tungnx checked has use
		 */
function duplicator() {
			$nonce = LP_Request::get_param( 'nonce' );
			if ( ! wp_verify_nonce( $nonce, 'wp_rest' ) ) {
				learn_press_send_json_error( __( 'Nonce is invalid!', 'learnpress' ) );
			}

			$post_id   = intval( $_GET['id'] ?? 0 );
			$post_type = learn_press_get_post_type( $post_id );

			if ( ! $post_id ) {
				learn_press_send_json_error( __( 'Oops! ID not found', 'learnpress' ) );
			} else {
				$can_duplicate = apply_filters( 'learn-press/can-duplicate-course', true, $post_id, $post_type );
				if ( ! current_user_can( ADMIN_ROLE ) ) {
					$post_author = get_post_field( 'post_author', $post_id );
					if ( get_current_user_id() != $post_author ) {
						$can_duplicate = false;
					}
				}

				if ( ! $can_duplicate ) {
					learn_press_send_json_error( __( 'You cannot duplicate this item.', 'learnpress' ) );
				}

				$new_item_id    = '';
				$duplicate_args = apply_filters( 'learn-press/duplicate-post-args', array( 'post_status' => 'publish' ) );

				switch ( $post_type ) {
					case LP_COURSE_CPT:
						$curd        = new LP_Course_CURD();
						$new_item_id = $curd->duplicate(
							$post_id,
							array(
								'exclude_meta' => array(
									'order-pending',
									'order-processing',
									'order-completed',
									'order-cancelled',
									'order-failed',
									'count_enrolled_users',
									'_lp_sample_data',
									'_lp_retake_count',
								),
							)
						);
						break;
					case LP_LESSON_CPT:
						$curd        = new LP_Lesson_CURD();
						$new_item_id = $curd->duplicate( $post_id, $duplicate_args );
						break;
					case LP_QUIZ_CPT:
						$curd        = new LP_Quiz_CURD();
						$new_item_id = $curd->duplicate( $post_id, $duplicate_args );
						break;
					case LP_QUESTION_CPT:
						$curd        = new LP_Question_CURD();
						$new_item_id = $curd->duplicate( $post_id, $duplicate_args );
						 break;
					default:
						break;
				}

				if ( is_wp_error( $new_item_id ) ) {
					learn_press_send_json_error( __( 'Duplicate post failed. Please try again', 'learnpress' ) );
				} else {
					learn_press_send_json_success( admin_url( 'post.php?post=' . $new_item_id . '&action=edit' ) );
				}
			}
		}
```

function create_table_courses() {
			global $wpdb;

				$sql = "CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS {$this->lp_db->tb_lp_courses} (

					price_to_sort FLOAT,
					is_sale int(1) default 0,
					post_author bigint unsigned,
					post_date_gmt datetime,
					post_content LONGTEXT,
					post_title text not null,
					post_status varchar(20) default 'publish' not null,
					post_name varchar(200) default '',
					menu_order int default 0,
					lang varchar(20),
					PRIMARY KEY (ID),
					KEY post_title (post_title(191)),
					KEY post_status (post_status),
					KEY post_name (post_name(191)),
					KEY id_status (ID, post_status)
				) $collation";

				$rs = $wpdb->query( $sql );

				if ( $rs ) {
					update_option( 'tb_learnpress_courses', 'yes' );
				}
			}
```

create_table_courses() uses $wpdb->query() with a raw DDL string instead of dbDelta() for table creation, and the try block is missing its catch clause making it syntactically incomplete.

WPCS Compliance: severity medium (6/10) — Issue: The function uses excessive indentation (3 tabs for the try body) and the try block has no catch clause, which is syntactically invalid PHP.. Fix: Fix indentation to one tab per nesting level and add a catch block to handle exceptions.

SQL Safety: severity high (4/10) — Issue: create_table_courses() uses $wpdb->query() with a raw DDL string for CREATE TABLE. The correct WP pattern for table creation is dbDelta() from wp-admin/includes/upgrade.php, which safely handles schema upgrades and is idempotent.. Fix: Replace $wpdb->query( $sql ) with require_once ABSPATH . 'wp-admin/includes/upgrade.php'; dbDelta( $sql ); to use the WordPress-sanctioned table creation API.

Security: severity low (8/10) — Issue: The table name comes from $this->lp_db->tb_lp_courses which is an internal property, not user input; no injection risk from that source.. Fix: No security changes needed.

Performance: severity low (8/10) — Issue: create_table_courses() is called during plugin installation/upgrade, not on every request; performance is not a concern.. Fix: No performance changes needed.

WP API Usage: severity high (4/10) — Issue: Using $wpdb->query() for CREATE TABLE bypasses dbDelta() which is the WordPress standard for safe, upgrade-aware schema management. dbDelta() compares the desired schema to the existing table and only makes necessary changes.. Fix: Use dbDelta( $sql ) after requiring wp-admin/includes/upgrade.php; remove the $rs check and instead use dbDelta's return value to verify table creation.

Code Quality: severity high (4/10) — Issue: The try block in create_table_courses() is missing its catch clause, making this syntactically invalid PHP. There is also no finally clause; the try has no matching closing handler.. Fix: Add a catch( Exception $e ) { ... } block or remove the try/catch wrapper if exception handling is not needed.

Dependency Integrity: severity low (8/10) — Issue: Depends on $this->lp_db->tb_lp_courses for the table name; this is an internal LearnPress dependency.. Fix: No dependency changes needed.

i18n: severity low (8/10) — Issue: create_table_courses() is a database schema function with no user-facing strings; i18n is not applicable.. Fix: No i18n changes needed.

Accessibility: severity low (8/10) — Issue: create_table_courses() is a schema management function with no HTML output; accessibility is not applicable.. Fix: No accessibility changes needed.

Key observation: The missing catch clause makes this syntactically invalid, and the use of raw $wpdb->query() for CREATE TABLE bypasses WordPress's dbDelta() API which is designed specifically for safe, idempotent schema upgrades.

/**
		 * Enqueue admin scripts and styles.
		 *
		 * @param string $hook_suffix The current admin page.
		 * @return void
		 */
function admin_enqueue_scripts( $hook_suffix ) {
			global $post;

			// Enqueue admin scripts only if adding or editing a supported post type.
			if ( in_array( $hook_suffix, array( 'post.php', 'post-new.php' ), true ) && post_type_supports( get_post_type(), self::KEY ) ) {
				$endpoint_url = '';
				$use_rest_api = 0;

				if ( self::use_rest_api() ) {
					$endpoint_url = WPCOM_Liveblog_Rest_Api::build_endpoint_base() . $post->ID . '/post_state';
					$use_rest_api = 1;
				}

				// Load dependencies from .asset.php file generated by @wordpress/scripts.
				$asset_file   = include plugin_dir_path( __FILE__ ) . 'build/admin.asset.php';
				$dependencies = isset( $asset_file['dependencies'] ) ? $asset_file['dependencies'] : array();
				$version      = isset( $asset_file['version'] ) ? $asset_file['version'] : self::VERSION;

				wp_enqueue_style( self::KEY, plugins_url( 'build/admin.css', __FILE__ ), array(), $version );
				wp_enqueue_script( 'liveblog-admin', plugins_url( 'build/admin.js', __FILE__ ), $dependencies, $version, false );
				wp_localize_script(
					'liveblog-admin',
					'liveblog_admin_settings',
					array(
						'nonce_key'                    => self::NONCE_KEY,
						'nonce'                        => wp_create_nonce( self::NONCE_ACTION ),
						'error_message_template'       => __( 'Error {error-code}: {error-message}', 'liveblog' ),
						'short_error_message_template' => __( 'Error: {error-message}', 'liveblog' ),
						'use_rest_api'                 => $use_rest_api,
						'endpoint_url'                 => $endpoint_url,
					)
				);
				// Also set liveblog_settings for admin context (needed by webpack_public_path).
				wp_localize_script(
					'liveblog-admin',
					'liveblog_settings',
					array(
						'plugin_dir' => plugin_dir_url( __FILE__ ),
					)
				);
			}
		}

							'error'   => true,
							'message' => self::MESSAGE_ERROR_NOT_POST_ID,
						),
						400
					);
				}

				if ( empty( $_GET['service'] ) ) {
					return wp_send_json(
						array(
							'error'   => true,
							'message' => 'Informar o campo "service"',
						),
						400
					);
				}

				return $payloadsController->showPayloadCart(
					SanitizeHelper::apply( $_GET['post_id'] ),
					SanitizeHelper::apply( $_GET['service'] )
				);
			}
		);
	}
```

The method reads $_GET['post_id'] before AJAX hooks fire and uses the pre-captured value inside closures, bypassing per-request nonce verification. The wp_ajax_nopriv_ handler exposes a destroy action to unauthenticated users without any authentication or nonce check.

WPCS Compliance: severity medium (6/10) — Issue: Function name 'laodRoutesPayload' contains a typo ('laod' instead of 'load'). PHPDoc @return declares void but the function can return false. WPCS requires accurate @return types.. Fix: Rename to loadRoutesPayload() and fix @return to '@return void' since callers should not rely on the false return value.

SQL Safety: severity low (8/10) — Issue: No direct SQL queries are executed in this routing method; SQL safety is delegated to the controller layer.. Fix: No SQL changes required here; ensure controllers use $wpdb->prepare() for any queries.

Security: severity critical (2/10) — Issue: The $_GET['post_id'] value is read once at registration time and captured via closure, but each AJAX handler fires at request time without re-verifying a nonce. The wp_ajax_nopriv_get_payload handler allows unauthenticated access to show() with no authentication check. The wp_ajax_destroy_payload action has a nopriv variant missing but the destroy action has no nonce protection at all.. Fix: Inside each AJAX closure, call check_ajax_referer('melhor-envio-cotacao_payload', 'nonce') before processing. Re-read and sanitize $_GET['post_id'] inside each closure rather than capturing a pre-request value. Remove or restrict the unauthenticated nopriv handler for destructive actions.

Performance: severity low (8/10) — Issue: Registering four add_action() hooks conditionally based on $_GET state at init-time is unusual but not a material performance concern.. Fix: No performance changes required.

WP API Usage: severity medium (6/10) — Issue: AJAX handlers should verify nonces with check_ajax_referer() or wp_verify_nonce() at the start of each handler. The current code skips this entirely.. Fix: Add check_ajax_referer('melhor-envio-cotacao_payload', 'nonce') as the first statement inside each AJAX closure.

Code Quality: severity medium (6/10) — Issue: The $postId is captured at registration time from a superglobal, but AJAX callbacks fire at a different request lifecycle point. This makes the closure behavior depend on the registration-time HTTP context rather than the callback-time context.. Fix: Move the sanitization of $_GET['post_id'] inside each closure so it reads the current request value when the AJAX handler actually executes.

Dependency Integrity: severity low (8/10) — Issue: SanitizeHelper::apply() is a custom helper; its sanitization behavior is undocumented relative to WordPress standards.. Fix: Consider replacing SanitizeHelper::apply() with absint() for post IDs to make the intent explicit and WordPress-idiomatic.

i18n: severity medium (6/10) — Issue: The error message 'Informar o campo "service"' is hardcoded in Portuguese without __() wrapping, making it untranslatable for non-Portuguese users.. Fix: Wrap with __('Please provide the "service" field.', 'melhor-envio-cotacao') to support internationalization.

Accessibility: severity low (8/10) — Issue: This is a server-side AJAX routing method with no HTML output; accessibility does not apply.. Fix: No accessibility changes required.

Key observation: The critical security flaw is that all four AJAX handlers lack nonce verification, and the nopriv handler for show() exposes unauthenticated data access with no rate limiting or capability check.

[/REASONING]

/**
	 * Function to return the options
	 * (own hands, acknowledgment of receipt, collection, extra fees, extra time)
	 * on database of the Melhor Envio shipping methods
	 *
	 * @return array
	 */
function getOptionsShipments() {
		global $wpdb;

		$results = $wpdb->get_results(
            $wpdb->prepare(
                "SELECT * FROM $wpdb->options WHERE option_name like %s",
                '%' . $wpdb->esc_like( self::KEY_OPTIONS_METHOD_DATABASE ) . '%'
            )
        );
		if ( empty( $results ) ) {
			return array();
		}

		$options = array_map(
			function ( $option ) {
				if ( ! empty( $option->option_value ) ) {
					$data = unserialize( $option->option_value );
					if ( ! empty( $data['id'] ) ) {
						$data['code_modal'] = 'code_shiping_' . $data['id'];
						$data['code']       = $data['id'];
						return $data;
					}
				}
			},
			$results
		);

		$codeStore = hash( 'sha512', get_option( 'home' ) );

		$_SESSION[ Session::ME_KEY ][ $codeStore ]['melhorenvio_options'] = $options;

		return $options;
	}
```

function convertStringToPrimType($primitive_type, $value) {
    if (array_key_exists($primitive_type, $this->enum_data)) {
      return $value;
    } elseif (in_array($primitive_type, array("unsigned int", "int"))) {
      return intval($value);
    } elseif ($primitive_type === "bool") {
      return boolval($value);
    } elseif ($primitive_type === "float") {
      return floatval($value);
    } elseif ($primitive_type === "datetime") {
      return $value;
    } elseif ($primitive_type === "string") {
      return $value;
    } elseif ($primitive_type === "Object") {
      return $value;
    } else {
      if (ApiConfig::TYPE_CHECKER_STRICT_MODE) {
        throw new \Exception("Not a primitive type");
      } else {
        return $value;
      }
    }
  }

/**
     * @param WC_Order|null $wc_order
     * @param string $type
     *
     * @return array|Exception|CoreOrder|void
     * @throws WC_Data_Exception
     */
function process(WC_Order $wc_order = null, string $type = CheckoutTypes::TRANSPARENT_VALUE)
    {
        if (
            (!Utils::is_request_ajax() && !Utils::isCheckoutRequest())
            || Utils::server('REQUEST_METHOD') !== 'POST'
        ) {
            exit(0);
        }
        if (!$wc_order) {
            wp_send_json_error(__('Invalid order', 'woo-pagarme-payments'));
        }
        if (!isset($_POST[PaymentRequestInterface::PAGARME_PAYMENT_REQUEST_KEY])) {
            wp_send_json_error(__('Invalid payment request', 'woo-pagarme-payments'));
        }
        if ($type === CheckoutTypes::TRANSPARENT_VALUE) {
            $fields = $this->convertCheckoutObject($_POST[PaymentRequestInterface::PAGARME_PAYMENT_REQUEST_KEY]);
            $fields['recurrence_cycle'] = Subscription::getRecurrenceCycle();
            $this->formatFieldsWhenIsSubscription($fields, $wc_order);
            $attempts = intval($wc_order->get_meta('_pagarme_attempts') ?? 0) + 1;
            $wc_order->update_meta_data("_pagarme_attempts", $attempts);
            $response = $this->orders->create_order(
                $wc_order,
                $fields['payment_method'],
                $fields
            );

            $order = new Order($wc_order->get_id());
            $totalWithInstallments = $order->getTotalAmountByCharges();
            $order->update_meta(
                'pagarme_card_tax',
                $order->calculateInstallmentFee($totalWithInstallments, $wc_order->get_total())
            );
            $order->getWcOrder()->set_total($this->getTotalValue($wc_order, $totalWithInstallments));
            $order->update_meta('payment_method', $fields['payment_method']);
            $order->update_meta("attempts", $attempts);
            $this->addAuthenticationOnMetaData($order, $fields);
            if ($response instanceof CoreOrder) {
                do_action("on_pagarme_response",  $response);
                WC()->cart->empty_cart();
                $order->getWcOrder()->set_transaction_id($response->getPagarmeId()->getValue());
                $order->update_meta('pagarme_id', $response->getPagarmeId()->getValue());
                $order->update_meta('pagarme_status', $response->getStatus()->getStatus());
                $this->addInstallmentsOnMetaData($order, $fields);
                $order->update_meta('response_data', json_encode($response));
                $order->getWcOrder()->save();
                $order->update_by_pagarme_status($response->getStatus()->getStatus());
                return $response;
            }
            $order->update_meta('pagarme_status', 'failed');
            $order->update_by_pagarme_status('failed');
            $order->getWcOrder()->save();
            return $response;
        }
    }
```

/**
	 * Specifies data which should be serialized to JSON.
	 *
	 * @since 0.1.0
	 *
	 * @return Data Exports to be serialized by json_encode().

function jsonSerialize(): array {
		$data = $this->data;

		$data['elements'] = array_map(
			static function ( OD_Element $element ): array {
				return $element->jsonSerialize();
			},
			$this->get_elements()
		);

		return $data;
	}

/**
 * Add the dominant color metadata to the attachment.
 *
 * @since 1.0.0
 *
 * @param array|mixed $metadata      The attachment metadata.
 * @param int         $attachment_id The attachment ID.
 * @return array{ has_transparency?: bool, dominant_color?: string } $metadata The attachment metadata.
 */
function dominant_color_metadata( $metadata, int $attachment_id ): array {
	if ( ! is_array( $metadata ) ) {
		$metadata = array();
	}

	$dominant_color_data = dominant_color_get_dominant_color_data( $attachment_id );
	if ( ! is_wp_error( $dominant_color_data ) ) {
		if ( isset( $dominant_color_data['dominant_color'] ) ) {
			$metadata['dominant_color'] = $dominant_color_data['dominant_color'];
		}

		if ( isset( $dominant_color_data['has_transparency'] ) ) {
			$metadata['has_transparency'] = $dominant_color_data['has_transparency'];
		}
	}

	return $metadata;
}

function test_autopay_version() {
		$current_version = blue_media()->get_plugin_version();
		$remote_url      = 'https://api.wordpress.org/plugins/info/1.0/platnosci-online-blue-media.json';
		$response        = wp_remote_get( $remote_url, [ 'timeout' => 5 ] );

				Log_Entry::get_header_info(),
				__( "Unable to fetch the latest Autopay plugin version from the WordPress repository.",
					"bm-woocommerce" )
			);
		}
		$body = wp_remote_retrieve_body( $response );
		$data = json_decode( $body );
		if ( empty( $data ) || empty( $data->version ) ) {

		}
		$latest_version = $data->version;
		if ( version_compare( $current_version, $latest_version, '<' ) ) {
			return new Log_Entry(
				Log_Entry::LEVEL_WARNING,

				sprintf( __( "An update is available for the Autopay plugin. Current version: %s, latest version: %s.",

		}
	}

This constructor is flagged as an AJAX handler without nonce verification, but the constructor itself only loads settings — the nonce check failure belongs to the AJAX action method that calls this constructor. Within the constructor itself, the main defect is that `get_option()` is cast to object with no fallback for a missing or false option.

WPCS Compliance: severity medium (6/10) — Issue: Double-quoted string `"plugnmeet_settings"` should be single-quoted per WordPress Coding Standards. No PHPDoc block is present for the constructor.. Fix: Changed double quotes to single quotes for the option name string. Added PHPDoc block.

SQL Safety: severity low (8/10) — Issue: `PlugNmeetAjaxHelper::__construct` uses `get_option()` exclusively — no raw SQL queries.. Fix: No SQL remediation needed.

Security: severity high (4/10) — Issue: If `get_option( 'plugnmeet_settings' )` returns `false` (option not set), `(object) false` produces a `stdClass` with no properties, and subsequent AJAX methods on `$this->setting_params` will issue PHP notices or access undefined properties without error, potentially leaking default or empty credentials.. Fix: Added a fallback: `get_option( 'plugnmeet_settings', array() )` so that an unset option yields an empty stdClass rather than a cast of boolean false.

Performance: severity low (8/10) — Issue: `get_option()` is cached by WordPress object cache, so calling it in the constructor is efficient. No performance concern.. Fix: No performance changes needed.

WP API Usage: severity medium (6/10) — Issue: `get_option( 'plugnmeet_settings' )` without a default argument returns `false` when the option does not exist. The correct pattern is to provide a default: `get_option( 'plugnmeet_settings', array() )`.. Fix: Added `array()` as the default argument to `get_option()`.

Code Quality: severity medium (6/10) — Issue: Casting the raw `get_option()` result to `(object)` without validation means any data shape stored in the DB becomes the settings object. A better pattern validates the returned array before use.. Fix: Added `is_array()` check before casting: if result is not an array, fall back to empty array before objectification.

Dependency Integrity: severity low (8/10) — Issue: The constructor depends solely on `get_option()` which is a core WP function — no external dependencies to guard.. Fix: No dependency integrity changes needed.

i18n: severity low (8/10) — Issue: `__construct` produces no user-facing strings — no i18n issues apply.. Fix: No i18n remediation needed.

Accessibility: severity low (8/10) — Issue: `PlugNmeetAjaxHelper::__construct` is a data-loading constructor with no HTML output — accessibility concerns do not apply to its current surface area.. Fix: No accessibility remediation needed; if this class is later wired to an HTML renderer, add appropriate label/ARIA attributes at that site.

Key observation: The missing default argument to `get_option()` means a false return value is silently cast to an empty stdClass, which causes downstream AJAX methods to access undefined properties on `$this->setting_params` without a clear error.

function ajax_upload_image() {
		require_once dirname( __FILE__ ) . '/polldaddy-client.php';

		check_admin_referer( 'send-media' );

		$attach_id = $media_id = $user_code = 0;
		$name = $url = '';

		if ( isset( $_POST['attach-id'] ) )
			$attach_id = (int) $_POST['attach-id'];

		if ( isset( $_POST['media-id'] ) )
			$media_id = (int) $_POST['media-id'];

		if ( isset( $_POST['uc'] ) )
			$user_code = $_POST['uc'];

		if ( isset( $_POST['url'] ) )
			$url = $_POST['url'];

		$parts     = pathinfo( $url );
		$name      = preg_replace('/\?.*/', '', $parts['basename']);
		$polldaddy = new api_client( WP_POLLDADDY__PARTNERGUID, $user_code );
		$data      = '';
		
		if ( function_exists( 'is_private_blog' ) && is_private_blog() ) {
			$file_path = get_attached_file( $attach_id );
			$data = base64_encode( @file_get_contents($file_path) );
		}
		
		$response  = $polldaddy->upload_image( $name, $url, 'poll', ($media_id>1000?$media_id:0), $data );

		if ( is_a( $response, "PollDaddy_Media" ) )
			echo urldecode( $response->upload_result ).'||'.$media_id;
		die();
	}

ajax_upload_image() reads $_POST['uc'] and $_POST['url'] without sanitization, uses @file_get_contents() to read an arbitrary attached file path without validating it is within the uploads directory, and echoes urldecode($response->upload_result) without escaping.

WPCS Compliance: severity high (4/10) — Issue: Missing PHPDoc block entirely; single-statement if blocks without braces violate WPCS; @file_get_contents uses error-suppression operator (@) which is banned by WPCS; no @since tag.. Fix: Add /** @since x.x.x @return void */ docblock; add braces to all single-statement if blocks; remove @ error suppression and handle file read errors explicitly.

SQL Safety: severity low (8/10) — Issue: No SQL in this method.. Fix: No SQL changes required.

Security: severity critical (2/10) — Issue: $user_code = $_POST['uc'] is read without sanitization and passed to new api_client(); $url = $_POST['url'] is unsanitized and passed to pathinfo() and upload_image(); echo urldecode($response->upload_result) outputs API response content without escaping, creating potential XSS; @file_get_contents($file_path) reads an arbitrary filesystem path derived from $attach_id without validating the file is within wp-content/uploads.. Fix: Sanitize: $user_code = sanitize_text_field( wp_unslash( $_POST['uc'] ?? '' ) ); $url = esc_url_raw( wp_unslash( $_POST['url'] ?? '' ) ); Replace echo urldecode(...) with echo esc_url( $response->upload_result ) . '||' . absint( $media_id ); Validate file path with validate_file() before file_get_contents().

Performance: severity low (8/10) — Issue: base64_encode(file_get_contents()) for large media files could consume significant memory; this is only triggered for private blogs so the impact is limited.. Fix: Consider reading the file in chunks for large files, but this is an edge case for private blog image uploads.

WP API Usage: severity high (4/10) — Issue: @file_get_contents() reads a file directly instead of using the WordPress Filesystem API (WP_Filesystem); WordPress requires WP_Filesystem for file operations to support non-standard server configurations.. Fix: Replace with: global $wp_filesystem; WP_Filesystem(); $data = base64_encode( $wp_filesystem->get_contents( $file_path ) ); after validating $file_path.

Code Quality: severity medium (6/10) — Issue: check_admin_referer('send-media') verifies a nonce but does not check current_user_can('upload_files'); any admin-level nonce holder can trigger this AJAX, not just users with media upload capability.. Fix: Use: $client_path = plugin_dir_path( __FILE__ ) . 'polldaddy-client.php'; if ( file_exists( $client_path ) ) { require_once $client_path; } else { wp_die( -1 ); }

	 * sending anything back to the browser.

function init() {

		}

	}
```

		$update_parts = [];
		foreach ( $meta_data as $key => $value ) {
			if ( ! in_array( $key, $allowed_keys, true ) ) {

The function body is truncated mid-loop — it is missing the closing brace for the `foreach`, the INSERT query construction, and the final `$wpdb->query()` call. The column name should be injected via a backtick-escaped pattern rather than string interpolation.. Fix: Used `"`{$key}` = %d"` with the key validated against the allowlist. Since $key is already validated, backtick escaping makes the pattern explicit. Also completed the missing INSERT ... ON DUPLICATE KEY UPDATE query using `$wpdb->prepare()`.

Consolidating to a single INSERT query (which is the apparent intent) is the right approach.. Fix: Completed the function with a single `INSERT ... /**
	 * Check if post is indexable.
	 *
	 * @param int $post_id Post ID to check.
	 *
	 * @return boolean
	 */
function is_post_indexable( $post_id ) {
		if ( true === self::is_post_excluded( $post_id ) ) {
			return false;
		}

		$robots = self::is_post_meta_indexable( $post_id );
		if ( is_bool( $robots ) ) {
			return $robots;
		}

		$post_type = get_post_type( $post_id );
		$robots    = false === $robots ? Helper::get_settings( 'titles.robots_global' ) : Helper::get_settings( 'titles.pt_' . $post_type . '_robots' );

		return in_array( 'noindex', (array) $robots, true ) ? false : true;
	}
```

/**
	 * Retrieve a Database instance by table name.
	 *
	 * @param string $table_name A Database instance id.
	 *
	 * @return Database Database object instance.
	 */
function table( $table_name ) {
		global $wpdb;

		if ( empty( self::$instances ) || empty( self::$instances[ $table_name ] ) ) {
			self::$instances[ $table_name ] = new Query_Builder( $wpdb->prefix . $table_name );
		}

		return self::$instances[ $table_name ];
	}
```

/**
	 * Run the query and return the results.
	 *
	 * @param array $args The query arguments.
	 */
function run( $args = [] ) {
		global $wpdb;

		$output = isset( $args['output'] ) && $args['output'] ? $args['output'] : OBJECT;
		$type   = $args['type'] ?? '';
		$query  = $args['query'] ?? '';
		$index  = $args['index'] ?? '';

		$start_time = microtime( true );

		$this->last_query = $query;
		$this->reset();

		switch ( $type ) {
			case 'row':
				$results = $wpdb->get_row( $query, $output, $index );
				break;
			case 'col':
				$results = $wpdb->get_col( $query, $index );
				break;
			case 'query':
				$results = $wpdb->query( $query );
				break;
			case 'var':
				$results = $wpdb->get_var( $query );
				break;
			default:
				$results = $wpdb->get_results( $query, $output );
				break;
		}

		return apply_filters( 'rank_math/database/query/results', $results, $args, $start_time );
	}
```

			$billing_state = strtolower( str_replace( ' ', '', get_user_meta( $user_id, 'billing_state', true ) ) );

/**
	 * Get Remote URL.
	 *
	 * Makes two HTTP requests to the URL with a configurable delay between them
	 * to ensure the cache has time to populate Age headers.
	 *
	 * @access public
	 * @static
	 * @param string $url (default: '') - The URL to fetch.
	 * @return array|string Response array from wp_remote_get(), or 'fail' on error.
	 * @since 4.4.0
	 */
function remote_get( $url = '' ) {

		// Make sure it's not a bad entry.
		$url = esc_url( $url );

		// Allow URL translation for environments where the public URL isn't
		// reachable from the server (e.g., Docker containers).
		// The filter receives the URL and should return the internal URL to use.
		$internal_url = apply_filters( 'vhp_debug_check_url', $url );
		$internal_url = esc_url( $internal_url );

		$args = array(
			'timeout'     => 30,
			'redirection' => 10,
		);

		// If we're using a different internal URL, we need to send the Host header
		// to ensure the server handles the request correctly, and disable redirects
		// since the redirect would go to the unreachable public URL.
		// The cache headers we need are present on the redirect response itself.
		if ( $internal_url !== $url ) {
			$parsed = wp_parse_url( $url );
			if ( ! empty( $parsed['host'] ) ) {
				$args['headers'] = array(
					'Host' => $parsed['host'],
				);
			}
			// Disable redirects - the redirect would go to the public URL which
			// isn't reachable from this environment. We check headers on the
			// redirect response instead.
			$args['redirection'] = 0;
			$url                 = $internal_url;
		}

		// Lazy run twice to make sure we get a primed cache page.
		$response1 = wp_remote_get( $url, $args );

		// If this fails, we're going to assume bad things...
		if ( is_wp_error( $response1 ) ) {
			return 'fail';
		}

		// Because the 'Age' header is an important check, wait before fetching again.
		// This delay ensures the cache has time to populate the Age header.
		$cache_check_delay = (int) apply_filters( 'vhp_debug_cache_check_delay', 2 );
		if ( $cache_check_delay > 0 ) {
			sleep( $cache_check_delay );
		}

		$response2 = wp_remote_get( $url, $args );

		// And if this fails, we again assume badly.
		if ( is_wp_error( $response2 ) ) {
			return 'fail';
		}

		return $response2;
	}
```

Performance: score 4/10 — sleep() called for up to 2 seconds (or more if filter increases it) on every invocation — this blocks PHP execution and will cause timeout issues on production servers when called at scale.

WP API Usage: score 8/10 — Uses wp_remote_get(), is_wp_error(), apply_filters(), esc_url(), wp_parse_url() correctly following WP HTTP API conventions.

Code Quality: score 6/10 — Returning the string literal 'fail' instead of WP_Error for error conditions breaks callers expecting consistent return types. Two separate wp_remote_get() calls with a blocking sleep between them is a fragile cache priming strategy.

<wp_judge> Evaluate this WordPress code:

```php
/**
 * Wraps a callback (e.g. for an action or filter) to be measured and included in the Server-Timing header.
 *
 * @since 1.8.0
 *
 * @param callable $callback    The callback to wrap.
 * @param string   $metric_slug The metric slug to use within the Server-Timing header.
 * @param string   $access_cap  Capability required to view the metric. If this is a public metric, this needs to be
 *                              set to "exist".
 * @return Closure Callback function that will run $callback and measure its execution time once called.
 */
function perflab_wrap_server_timing( callable $callback, string $metric_slug, string $access_cap ): Closure {
	return static function ( ...$callback_args ) use ( $callback, $metric_slug, $access_cap ) {
		// Gain access to Perflab_Server_Timing_Metric instance.
		$server_timing_metric = null;

		// Only register the metric the first time the function is called.
		// For now, this also means only the first function call is measured.
		if ( ! perflab_server_timing()->has_registered_metric( $metric_slug ) ) {
			perflab_server_timing_register_metric(
				$metric_slug,
				array(
					'measure_callback' => static function ( $metric ) use ( &$server_timing_metric ): void {
						$server_timing_metric = $metric;
					},
					'access_cap'       => $access_cap,
				)
			);
		}

		// If metric instance was not set, this metric should not be calculated.
		if ( null === $server_timing_metric ) {
			return call_user_func_array( $callback, $callback_args );
		}

		// Measure time before the callback.
		$server_timing_metric->measure_before();

		// Execute the callback.
		$result = call_user_func_array( $callback, $callback_args );

		// Measure time after the callback and calculate total.
		$server_timing_metric->measure_after();

		// Return result (e.g. in case this is a filter callback).
		return $result;
	};
}
```

WPCS Compliance: score 9/10 — Complete docblock with @since 1.8.0, typed parameters, Closure return type declaration, proper array alignment with spaces, snake_case naming, and inline comments per WPCS standards throughout.

SQL Safety: score None/10 — No $wpdb usage or SQL queries present; this is a pure timing instrumentation wrapper.

Security: score 8/10 — Uses $access_cap to gate metric visibility, which is correctly passed through to perflab_server_timing_register_metric(); static closures prevent unintended $this capture; call_user_func_array is used safely with internally-sourced $callback.

Performance: score 8/10 — has_registered_metric() guard prevents duplicate metric registration on repeated calls; measure_before/measure_after pattern is minimal overhead; call_user_func_array introduces minor overhead vs direct call but is necessary for variadic forwarding.

WP API Usage: score 9/10 — Correctly uses perflab_server_timing() and perflab_server_timing_register_metric() — the plugin's own public API — with proper 'measure_callback' and 'access_cap' keys matching the registration contract.

Code Quality: score 9/10 — Higher-order function pattern is well-structured; reference capture via &$server_timing_metric for the inner closure is the correct approach; inline comments explain each step; defensive null-check before measurement is thorough.

Dependency Integrity: score 8/10 — Depends on perflab_server_timing() and perflab_server_timing_register_metric() which are defined within the performance-lab plugin; no external library dependencies.

i18n: score None/10 — No user-facing strings present; function is a pure instrumentation wrapper with no output.

Accessibility: score 3/10 — No direct HTML output from this function, but it wraps callbacks that may produce HTML output; the Server-Timing header it populates is a dev/diagnostic tool without accessibility implications — honest low score rather than third null.

        if (!file_exists(Paths::getConfigFileName())) {
            $result[] = 'Hold on. You need to save options before you can run this test. There is no config file yet.';
            return [true, $result];
        }

        if ($this->config['image-types'] == 0) {
            $result[] = 'No image types have been activated, nothing to test';
            return [true, $result];
        }

        foreach ($this->config['scope'] as $rootId) {
            list($success, $subResult) = $this->runTestForRoot($rootId);
            $result = array_merge($result, $subResult);
        }

        return [$success, $result];
    }

/**
	 * Get product from api args.
	 *
	 * @return array.
	 * @throws \Exception
	 */
function get_shipping_product() {
		$checked_features = Utils::get_selected_label_features( $this->api_args['backend_data'] );
		$shipping_feature = $this->get_selected_shipping_features();
		$from_country     = $this->api_args['store_address']['country'];
		$to_country       = $this->api_args['shipping_address']['country'];
		$to_state         = $this->api_args['shipping_address']['state'];

		$features = array_keys( $checked_features );
		$code_map = Mapping::products_data();

		$selected_product = array();
		$destination      = Utils::get_shipping_zone( $to_country, $to_state );
		if ( empty( $code_map[ $from_country ][ $destination ][ $shipping_feature ] ) ) {
			return $selected_product;
		}
		foreach ( $code_map[ $from_country ][ $destination ][ $shipping_feature ] as $product ) {
			if ( empty( $product['combination'] ) && empty( $selected_product ) ) {
				$selected_product = $product;
				continue;
			}
			$is_this_it = true;
			foreach ( $features as $feature ) {
				if ( ! in_array( $feature, $product['combination'] ) ) {
					$is_this_it = false;
				}
			}
			foreach ( $product['combination'] as $combination ) {
				if ( ! in_array( $combination, $features ) ) {
					$is_this_it = false;
				}
			}

			if ( $is_this_it ) {
				$selected_product = $product;
			}
		}

		return $selected_product;
	}
```

function fs_request_get_raw( $key, $def = false, $type = false ) {
            if ( is_string( $type ) ) {
                $type = strtolower( $type );
            }

            switch ( $type ) {
                case 'post':
                    // phpcs:ignore WordPress.Security.NonceVerification.Missing
                    $value = isset( $_POST[ $key ] ) ? $_POST[ $key ] : $def;
                    break;
                case 'get':
                    // phpcs:ignore WordPress.Security.NonceVerification.Recommended
                    $value = isset( $_GET[ $key ] ) ? $_GET[ $key ] : $def;
                    break;
                default:
                    // phpcs:ignore WordPress.Security.NonceVerification.Recommended
                    $value = isset( $_REQUEST[ $key ] ) ? $_REQUEST[ $key ] : $def;
                    break;
            }

            return empty( $value ) ? $value : wp_unslash( $value );
        }
```

function get_customer_currency_args_from_request(): array {

		];

		if ( isset( $_GET['currency_is'] ) && is_array( $_GET['currency_is'] ) ) {

			$args['currency'] = sanitize_text_field( wp_unslash( $_GET['currency'] ) );

	}

/**
	 * Modified version of parse() from KeepAChangelogParser.
	 *
	 * @param string $changelog Changelog contents.
	 * @return Changelog
	 * @throws InvalidArgumentException If the changelog data cannot be parsed.
	 */
function parse( $changelog ) {
		$ret = new Changelog();

		// Fix newlines and expand tabs.
		$changelog = strtr( $changelog, [ "\r\n" => "\n" ] );
		$changelog = strtr( $changelog, [ "\r" => "\n" ] );
		while ( strpos( $changelog, "\t" ) !== false ) {
			$changelog = preg_replace_callback(
				'/^([^\t\n]*)\t/m',
				function ( $m ) {
					return $m[1] . str_repeat( ' ', 4 - ( mb_strlen( $m[1] ) % 4 ) );
				},
				$changelog
			);
		}

		// Remove title.
		list( $first_line, $remaining ) = explode( "\n", $changelog, 2 );
		if ( false !== strpos( $first_line, $this->title ) ) {
			$changelog = $remaining;
		}

		$entries = [];
		preg_match_all( '/^=\s+([^\n=]+)\s+=(((?!^=).)+)/ms', $changelog, $version_sections );

		foreach ( $version_sections[0] as $section ) {
			$heading_pattern = '/^= +(\[?[^] ]+\]?) - (.+?) =/';
			preg_match( $heading_pattern, $section, $heading );
			if ( ! count( $heading ) ) {
				throw new InvalidArgumentException( "Invalid heading: $heading" );
			}

			$version   = $heading[1];
			$timestamp = $heading[2];
			if ( $timestamp === $this->get_unreleased_date() ) {
				$timestamp       = null;
				$entry_timestamp = new DateTime( 'now', new DateTimeZone( 'UTC' ) );
			} else {
				try {
					$timestamp = new DateTime( $timestamp, new DateTimeZone( 'UTC' ) );
				} catch ( \Exception $ex ) {
					throw new InvalidArgumentException( "Heading has an invalid timestamp: $heading", 0, $ex );
				}
				if ( strtotime( $heading[2], 0 ) !== strtotime( $heading[2], 1000000000 ) ) {
					throw new InvalidArgumentException( "Heading has a relative timestamp: $heading" );
				}
				$entry_timestamp = $timestamp;
			}

			$entry = $this->newChangelogEntry(
				$version,
				[
					'timestamp' => $timestamp,
				]
			);

			$entries[] = $entry;
			$content   = trim( preg_replace( $heading_pattern, '', $section ) );

			if ( '' === $content ) {
				continue;
			}

			while ( '' !== $content ) {
				$changes = [];
				$rows    = explode( "\n", $content );
				foreach ( $rows as $row ) {
					$row          = trim( $row );
					$row          = preg_replace( '/\\' . $this->bullet . '/', '', $row, 1 );
					$row_segments = explode( $this->separator, $row, 2 );

					if ( count( $row_segments ) !== 2 ) {
						throw new Exception( 'Change entry does not have the correct format. Please update it manually and run this command again. Change entry: ' . $row );
					}

					array_push(
						$changes,
						[
							'subheading' => trim( $row_segments[0] ),
							'content'    => trim( $row_segments[1] ),
						]
					);
				}

				foreach ( $changes as $change ) {
					$entry->appendChange(
						$this->newChangeEntry(
							[
								'subheading' => $change['subheading'],
								'content'    => $change['content'],
								'timestamp'  => $entry_timestamp,
							]
						)
					);
				}
				$content = '';
			}
		}

		$ret->setEntries( $entries );

		return $ret;
	}
```

This developer-tool parser method interpolates an array variable $heading directly into exception message strings, producing 'Array' as output rather than meaningful information. The inner while loop is also structurally redundant — it always runs exactly once, making it misleading dead structure.

/**
	 * Tries to determine the current WC Order Id based on the PayPal order
	 * and the current order in session.
	 *
	 * @param int|null $value The initial value.
	 *
	 * @return int|null
	 */
function determine_wc_order_id( ?int $value = null ) {

		if ( ! isset( $_REQUEST['ppcp-resume-order'] ) ) {
			return $value;
		}

		$resume_order_id = (int) WC()->session->get( 'order_awaiting_payment' );

		$order = $this->session_handler->order();
		if ( ! $order ) {
			return $value;
		}

		$order_id = false;
		foreach ( $order->purchase_units() as $purchase_unit ) {
			if ( $purchase_unit->custom_id() === sanitize_text_field( wp_unslash( $_REQUEST['ppcp-resume-order'] ) ) ) {
				$order_id = (int) $purchase_unit->custom_id();
			}
		}
		if ( $order_id === $resume_order_id ) {
			$value = $order_id;
		}
		return $value;
	}
```

/**
	 * Determines if an order is ready for capture.
	 *
	 * The base implementation of this method checks for a valid order status and that a transaction ID is set.
	 *
	 * @since 3.0.0
	 *
	 * @param \WC_Order $order order object
	 * @return bool
	 */
function is_order_ready_for_capture( \WC_Order $order ) {

		return ! in_array( $order->get_status(), array( 'cancelled', 'refunded', 'failed' ), true ) && $this->get_gateway()->get_order_meta( $order, 'trans_id' );
	}

/**
	 * Get the connected user's subscriptions.
	 *
	 * @return array
	 *
	 * phpcs:ignore Squiz.Commenting.FunctionCommentThrowTag.Missing -- As we wrap the throw in a try/catch.
	 */
function get_subscriptions() {
		$cache_key = '_woocommerce_helper_subscriptions';
		$data      = get_transient( $cache_key );
		if ( false !== $data ) {
			if ( is_array( $data ) ) {
				return $data;
			}
			// Cached data is corrupted, delete and fetch fresh.
			delete_transient( $cache_key );
		}

		try {
			$request_uri = wp_unslash( $_SERVER['REQUEST_URI'] ?? '' ); // phpcs:ignore WordPress.Security.ValidatedSanitizedInput.InputNotSanitized
			$source      = '';
			if ( false !== stripos( $request_uri, 'wc/v3/marketplace/refresh' ) ) :
				$source = 'refresh-button';
			elseif ( false !== stripos( $request_uri, 'my-subscriptions' ) ) :
				$source = 'my-subscriptions';
			elseif ( false !== stripos( $request_uri, 'plugins.php' ) ) :
				$source = 'plugins';
			elseif ( false !== stripos( $request_uri, 'wc-admin' ) ) :
				$source = 'inbox-notes';
			elseif ( false !== stripos( $request_uri, 'admin-ajax.php' ) ) :
				$source = 'heartbeat-api';
			elseif ( false !== stripos( $request_uri, 'wc/v3/marketplace' ) ) :
				$source = 'wccom-site-installer';
			elseif ( defined( 'WP_CLI' ) && WP_CLI ) :
				$source = 'wc-cli';
			endif;

			// Obtain the connected user info.
			$request = WC_Helper_API::get(
				'subscriptions',
				array(
					'authenticated' => true,
					'query_string'  => '' !== $source ? esc_url( '?source=' . $source ) : '',
				)
			);

			if ( is_wp_error( $request ) ) {
				set_transient( $cache_key, array(), 15 * MINUTE_IN_SECONDS );

				throw new Exception( $request->get_error_message(), (int) $request->get_error_data() );
			}

			$code = wp_remote_retrieve_response_code( $request );
			if ( 200 !== $code ) {
				set_transient( $cache_key, array(), 15 * MINUTE_IN_SECONDS );
				throw new Exception( self::get_message_for_response_code( $code ), $code );
			}

			$data = json_decode( wp_remote_retrieve_body( $request ), true );
			if ( ! is_array( $data ) ) {
				set_transient( $cache_key, array(), 15 * MINUTE_IN_SECONDS );
				throw new Exception( __( 'WooCommerce.com API returned an invalid response.', 'woocommerce' ), 422 );
			}

			set_transient( $cache_key, $data, 3 * HOUR_IN_SECONDS );

			self::remove_api_error_notice();
			return $data;
		} catch ( Exception $e ) {
			if ( $e->getCode() < 404 ) {
				self::remove_api_error_notice();
			} else {
				self::log( 'Error getting subscriptions: ' . $e->getMessage(), 'error' );
				self::add_api_error_notice();
			}
		}

		return array();
	}
```

WPCS Compliance: score 7/10 — PHPCS ignore inline comment is properly formatted with justification. Tab indentation and spacing are consistent throughout. The colon-style if/elseif/endif is valid WPCS alternative syntax. The code note about the 'installer' source label in the git status vs 'wccom-site-installer' in the actual code is a non-issue here.

Code Quality: score 7/10 — Cache corruption detection is a nice defensive touch. The elseif chain for source detection is lengthy but readable. The catch block's error code threshold (< 404) is a clear business rule. Redundant set_transient() calls on error paths could be extracted to a helper.
